title: bugfix.T2: temporal validation split — fix cross-series residual contamination (R2)
description:
# bugfix.T2: Temporal block validation split (final spec)

## I. Objective
Replace random stacked-row split with time-block split; preserve min_validation_rows guard.

## II. Bug
sample.int(n_total, n_valid) → cross-series temporal contamination in residuals.

## III. Seed
set.seed(seed) at line 108 ONLY controlled sample.int. After fix, seed is no-op for validation split (deterministic). Seed at line ~578 (chunk_strategy="random") is UNAFFECTED — leave it. seed param stays in signature.

## IV. Logic — replace validation split block (~lines 94-114)
```r
if (validation_split > 0) {
  n_per_series <- max(1L, round(validation_split * T_obs))
  if (n_per_series >= T_obs) {
    cli_abort(
      "`validation_split` too large: all {T_obs} rows used as validation, none for training.",
      call = NULL
    )
  }
  # Temporal block: last n_per_series rows from each series block (series-major stack)
  valid_idx <- unlist(lapply(seq_len(p), function(j) {
    block_end <- j * T_obs
    (block_end - n_per_series + 1L):block_end
  }))
  n_valid_total <- n_per_series * p
  # Preserve min_validation_rows guard (disable early stopping on tiny sets)
  if (n_valid_total < min_validation_rows) {
    cli_warn(
      c("Validation set has only {n_valid_total} row{?s} (< {min_validation_rows}).",
        "i" = "Early stopping disabled."),
      call = NULL
    )
    valid_idx <- integer(0L)
    train_idx <- seq_len(n_total)
  } else {
    train_idx <- setdiff(seq_len(n_total), valid_idx)
  }
} else {
  train_idx <- seq_len(n_total)
  valid_idx <- integer(0L)
}
```

Also in compute_rec_residuals (R/utils.R): derive T_valid from valid_idx rather than resid_vec:
```r
n_valid_per_series <- length(fit_obj$valid_idx) %/% p
# integer-division guard kept as safety net
```

## VI. DoD
- [ ] temporal block split active
- [ ] min_validation_rows guard preserved and emits cli_warn
- [ ] cli_abort on validation_split too large
- [ ] seed removed from validation path; chunk seed unaffected
- [ ] FAIL 0; conventional commit

title: bugfix.T1: sort series_id_levels in batch path (S2)
description:
# bugfix.T1: Fix unsorted series_id_levels in chunked path

## I. Objective
Fix factor level ordering mismatch between .run_chunked_rml_g() (unsorted) and .stack_series() (sorted).

## II. Bug location
R/rml_g.R line ~677: `series_id_levels = colnames(obs)` — unsorted.
.stack_series() line 53: `sort(unique(as.character(series_names)))` — sorted.
Interop fails when a batch-fitted rml_g_fit is used for predict alongside a standard-fitted one.

## III. Fix
Change line ~677 to: `series_id_levels = sort(unique(as.character(colnames(obs)))),`

## IV. Logic
1. grep -n "series_id_levels.*colnames" R/rml_g.R
2. Edit single line
3. R CMD INSTALL --preclean . 2>&1 | tail -2
4. devtools::test() FAIL 0
5. Commit: `fix(rml_g): sort series_id_levels in chunked path (S2)`

## VI. DoD
- [ ] Line uses sort(unique(as.character(colnames(obs))))
- [ ] FAIL 0; conventional commit
close_reason: duplicate of eo2.1

title: bugfix.T1: sort series_id_levels in batch path (S2)
description:
# bugfix.T1: series_id_levels sort in .run_chunked_rml_g()

## I. Objective
Fix factor level ordering mismatch between batch path (unsorted) and standard path (sorted).

## II. Bug
R/rml_g.R line 677:
```r
series_id_levels = colnames(obs),  # BUG: unsorted
```
.stack_series() line 53 sorts: `sort(unique(as.character(series_names)))`.
A batch-fit rml_g_fit cannot interoperate with a standard-fit rml_g_fit because factor codings differ.

## III. Fix
```r
series_id_levels = sort(unique(as.character(colnames(obs)))),
```

## IV. Logic
1. `grep -n "series_id_levels.*colnames" R/rml_g.R` — confirm line 677
2. Edit to add sort(unique(as.character(...)))
3. R CMD INSTALL --preclean . clean
4. devtools::test() FAIL 0
5. Commit: `fix(rml_g): sort series_id_levels in chunked path (S2)`

## VI. DoD
- [ ] Line 677 uses sort(unique(as.character(colnames(obs))))
- [ ] FAIL 0
- [ ] Conventional commit

title: ak9.8a: rewrite make_g_fixture into 3 framework-specific fixtures (cs/te/ct)
description:
# ak9.12: Rewrite test fixtures into 3 framework-specific variants

## I. Context & Objective
- **Objective:** Replace broken `make_g_fixture()` (ncol_hat=8 but n=6, incompatible) with `make_g_fixture_cs/te/ct()` matching each framework's actual column space.
- **Why:** Precondition for ak9.2/3/4. Wrappers will enforce ncol(base)==ncol(hat); current fixture would fail this guard.
- **Reference:** docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-15-rml-g-reconcile-api-design.md §Test Fixtures.

## II. Input Specification
- **File:** tests/testthat/test-rml-g-wrappers.R
- **Replace:** `make_g_fixture(p, T_obs, ncol_hat)` (lines 3-13)
- **Format:** R function definitions; named list returns.

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
|------|-------|
| Logic | ncol(hat) == ncol(base) per framework (invariant for new API) |
| Format | Three distinct functions, NOT a single parameterized fixture |
| Boundary | Do NOT change any existing test_that() bodies in this ticket; only ak9.13 does that |
| Forbidden | Do NOT delete tests; they may temporarily fail until ak9.13 fixes them |

## IV. Step-by-Step Logic
1. Delete current `make_g_fixture()` (lines 3-13).
2. Add three new fixtures:

```r
make_g_fixture_cs <- function(p = 4L, T_obs = 20L, h = 2L) {
  set.seed(99)
  na <- 2L
  n <- na + p
  agg_mat <- matrix(c(1,1,0,0,0,0,1,1), nrow = na, ncol = p,
                    dimnames = list(c("G1","G2"), paste0("S", seq_len(p))))
  obs <- matrix(rnorm(T_obs * p), T_obs, p,
                dimnames = list(NULL, paste0("S", seq_len(p))))
  hat <- matrix(rnorm(T_obs * n), T_obs, n,
                dimnames = list(NULL, c(rownames(agg_mat), colnames(obs))))
  base <- matrix(rnorm(h * n), h, n,
                 dimnames = list(NULL, colnames(hat)))
  list(agg_mat = agg_mat, obs = obs, hat = hat, base = base)
}

make_g_fixture_te <- function(T_obs = 60L, h = 2L, agg_order = c(4L, 2L, 1L)) {
  set.seed(99)
  m  <- max(agg_order)
  kt <- sum(m / agg_order)
  hat  <- matrix(rnorm(T_obs * kt), T_obs, kt,
                 dimnames = list(NULL, paste0("L", seq_len(kt))))
  obs  <- matrix(rnorm(T_obs),  T_obs, 1L,
                 dimnames = list(NULL, "S1"))
  base <- matrix(rnorm(h * m * kt), h * m, kt,
                 dimnames = list(NULL, colnames(hat)))
  list(agg_order = agg_order, obs = obs, hat = hat, base = base)
}

make_g_fixture_ct <- function(p = 2L, T_obs = 60L, h = 2L, agg_order = c(4L, 2L, 1L)) {
  set.seed(99)
  m   <- max(agg_order)
  kt  <- sum(m / agg_order)
  na  <- 1L
  n   <- na + p
  ncf <- n * kt
  agg_mat <- matrix(c(1, 1), nrow = na, ncol = p,
                    dimnames = list("G1", paste0("S", seq_len(p))))
  obs  <- matrix(rnorm(T_obs * p), T_obs, p,
                 dimnames = list(NULL, paste0("S", seq_len(p))))
  hat  <- matrix(rnorm(T_obs * ncf), T_obs, ncf,
                 dimnames = list(NULL, paste0("F", seq_len(ncf))))
  base <- matrix(rnorm(h * m * ncf), h * m, ncf,
                 dimnames = list(NULL, colnames(hat)))
  list(agg_mat = agg_mat, agg_order = agg_order, obs = obs, hat = hat, base = base)
}
```

3. Update calls in existing tests:
   - csrml_g tests → `make_g_fixture_cs()`
   - terml_g tests → `make_g_fixture_te()`
   - ctrml_g tests (if any) → `make_g_fixture_ct()`

4. Run: `Rscript -e 'devtools::test(filter="rml-g-wrappers")' 2>&1 | tail -20` — expect failures only on `expect_s3_class(result, "rml_g_fit")` assertions (those die in ak9.13).

5. Commit: `test(fixtures): split make_g_fixture into framework-specific cs/te/ct variants (ak9.12)`

## V. Output Schema (Strict)
```toon
task_id: FoRecoML-ak9.12
success: bool
data:
  files_changed: [tests/testthat/test-rml-g-wrappers.R]
  fixture_funcs_added: [make_g_fixture_cs, make_g_fixture_te, make_g_fixture_ct]
  test_run: pass_count: <N>, fail_count: <N>, fail_reason: "expect_s3_class only (expected)"
error_log: null | msg
```

## VI. Definition of Done
- [ ] make_g_fixture() removed
- [ ] 3 new fixture functions present
- [ ] All test_that() calls updated to use the right framework fixture
- [ ] R CMD INSTALL --preclean . passes
- [ ] Test failures only on expect_s3_class assertions (will be fixed in ak9.13)
- [ ] Conventional commit with ticket ID
close_reason: 3 framework-specific fixtures landed; tests pass except 1 expected ak9.13 assertion drift

title: feat(utils): add apply_norm_params() helper — apply stored normalize_stack params to new data
description:
## Objective
Add `apply_norm_params(X_new, norm_params)` to R/utils.R. Applies stored center/scale from normalize_stack() to new data. Internal only, not exported.

## Context — actual normalize_stack() return structure
R/utils.R:461–479: normalize_stack() returns list(X_norm=<matrix>, center=<numeric vector>, scale=<numeric vector>). Fields are named `center` and `scale` — NOT mu/sigma. The *_g wrappers store the full return value: `fit_obj$norm_params <- nr` (rml_g.R:668/756/850).

## Input
- `X_new`: numeric matrix, ncol = ncol_hat
- `norm_params`: the list returned by normalize_stack() — has fields `$center` (numeric vector, length=ncol) and `$scale` (numeric vector, length=ncol). NULL = no normalization.

## Output
Numeric matrix, same shape as X_new.

## Exact logic
```r
apply_norm_params <- function(X_new, norm_params) {
  if (is.null(norm_params)) return(X_new)
  X_new <- sweep(X_new, 2, norm_params$center, "-")
  X_new <- sweep(X_new, 2, norm_params$scale, "/")
  X_new
}
```

## base → newdata shape transformation (for ak9.2/3/4)
For global *_g functions:
- `base` is passed by user as `h × n` matrix (h forecast horizons, n = all series including upper levels)
- This is used DIRECTLY as `newdata` to predict.rml_g_fit — no reshaping needed
- `h` observations × `n` feature columns matches the training layout (hat was T_obs × n)
- predict.rml_g_fit with series_id=NULL broadcasts predictions for each training series level
- Output is a numeric vector of length h × nb (nb = bottom-level series count)
- Wrappers (ak9.2/3/4) reshape this vector into matrix and pass to csbu/tebu/ctbu

## File
R/utils.R — append after normalize_stack() (currently line 479). Do NOT export.

## Guards
- Use `norm_params$center` and `norm_params$scale` — not mu/sigma (those fields do not exist)
- If norm_params is NULL, return X_new unchanged (identity)
- Do NOT call normalize_stack() inside this function

## DoD
- Function exists in R/utils.R using `$center`/`$scale` field names
- R CMD INSTALL --preclean . passes
- ak9.8 (E1) test verifies: apply_norm_params(X, normalize_stack(X)$X_norm) ... actually: nr <- normalize_stack(X_train); X_normed <- apply_norm_params(X_new, nr) — result has same shape as X_new
close_reason: apply_norm_params() helper implemented, committed, reviewed

title: A3: ctrml_g — fit + predict + ctbu + return reconciled matrix
description:
# ak9.4: ctrml_g — fit + apply_norm_params + predict + ctbu + return matrix

## I. Context & Objective
- **Objective:** Replace ctrml_g's bare return with full fit+predict+ctbu pipeline.
- **Why:** Same bug for cross-temporal framework.
- **Reference:** docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-15-rml-g-reconcile-api-design.md §ctrml_g pipeline.

## II. Input Specification
- **File:** R/rml_g.R, function ctrml_g
- **Format:** modify function body; signature unchanged

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
|------|-------|
| Logic | ncol(base) == ncol(hat) |
| Logic | nrow(base) %% max(agg_order) == 0 |
| Logic | ctbu expects nb × h_hf; reshape predict output and transpose |
| Logic | Output column count = h × kt (NOT h × max(agg_order)) — kt = sum(max(agg_order)/agg_order) |
| Format | Return matrix n × (h × kt) with attr("FoReco") |
| Forbidden | bare new_foreco_info (no triple-colon) |

## IV. Step-by-Step Logic
1. Read current ctrml_g body.
2. Add guards at function START:
```r
if (missing(base)) cli_abort("Argument {.arg base} is missing.", call = NULL)
base <- as.matrix(base)
if (!is.numeric(base)) cli_abort("{.arg base} must be numeric.", call = NULL)
if (ncol(base) != ncol(hat)) cli_abort(
  "{.arg base} must have {ncol(hat)} columns; got {ncol(base)}.", call = NULL)
m <- max(agg_order)
if (nrow(base) %% m != 0) cli_abort(
  "{.arg base} must have rows divisible by max(agg_order) = {m}; got {nrow(base)}.",
  call = NULL)
```
3. After fitting:
```r
fit_obj$ncol_hat <- ncol(hat)
```
4. Replace return(fit_obj) with:
```r
base_features <- apply_norm_params(base, fit_obj$norm_params)
bts_vec <- predict(fit_obj, newdata = base_features)
h_hf <- nrow(base)
nb   <- length(fit_obj$series_id_levels)
bts_mat <- matrix(bts_vec, nrow = h_hf, ncol = nb)
reco_mat <- FoReco::ctbu(t(bts_mat),
                         agg_mat = fit_obj$agg_mat,
                         agg_order = fit_obj$agg_order)
attr(reco_mat, "FoReco") <- new_foreco_info(list(
  fit = fit_obj, framework = "Cross-temporal",
  forecast_horizon = h_hf / m, rfun = "ctrml_g", ml = approach))
reco_mat
```
5. Update @return: "numeric matrix n × (h × kt) where kt = sum(max(agg_order)/agg_order), with attr('FoReco')."
6. R CMD INSTALL --preclean . — clean.
7. Smoke test:
```bash
Rscript -e '
library(FoRecoML); set.seed(1)
agg_order <- c(4L, 2L, 1L); m <- 4L; kt <- 7L
p <- 2L; na <- 1L; n <- na + p; ncf <- n * kt
T_obs <- 60L; h <- 2L
agg_mat <- matrix(c(1,1), nrow=na, ncol=p); dimnames(agg_mat) <- list("G1", c("S1","S2"))
hat <- matrix(rnorm(T_obs*ncf), T_obs, ncf); colnames(hat) <- paste0("F", 1:ncf)
obs <- matrix(rnorm(T_obs*p), T_obs, p); colnames(obs) <- c("S1","S2")
base <- matrix(rnorm(h*m*ncf), h*m, ncf); colnames(base) <- paste0("F", 1:ncf)
r <- ctrml_g(base=base, hat=hat, obs=obs, agg_mat=agg_mat, agg_order=agg_order, seed=1L)
stopifnot(is.matrix(r), !is.null(attr(r, "FoReco")))
cat("OK dim=", dim(r), "expected: c(n, h*kt) =", c(n, h*kt), "\n")
'
```
8. Commit: `fix(rml_g): ctrml_g — restore fit+predict+ctbu pipeline; return n×(h×kt) matrix (ak9.4)`

## V. Output Schema (Strict)
```toon
task_id: FoRecoML-ak9.4
success: bool
data:
  files_changed: [R/rml_g.R]
  function: ctrml_g
  smoke_test:
    is_matrix: bool
    has_foreco_attr: bool
    dim_matches_n_by_h_kt: bool
error_log: null | msg
```

## VI. Definition of Done
- [ ] ctrml_g body uses pipeline
- [ ] Guards in place
- [ ] fit_obj$ncol_hat set
- [ ] bare new_foreco_info
- [ ] @return updated to n × (h × kt)
- [ ] Smoke test passes with correct dim
- [ ] Conventional commit
close_reason: ctrml_g pipeline restored; smoke test dim=3x14 matches n×(h*kt)

title: A2: terml_g — fit + predict + tebu + return reconciled matrix
description:
# ak9.3: terml_g — fit + apply_norm_params + predict + tebu + return named vector

## I. Context & Objective
- **Objective:** Replace terml_g's bare `return(fit_obj)` with fit+predict+tebu pipeline returning a NAMED numeric vector.
- **Why:** Same bug as ak9.2 for temporal framework.
- **Reference:** docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-15-rml-g-reconcile-api-design.md §terml_g pipeline.

## II. Input Specification
- **File:** R/rml_g.R, function terml_g
- **Format:** modify function body in-place; signature unchanged

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
|------|-------|
| Logic | ncol(obs) == 1L invariant (multi-series is ctrml_g) |
| Logic | nrow(base) %% max(agg_order) == 0 (temporal periodicity) |
| Logic | ncol(base) == ncol(hat) (same as csrml_g) |
| Logic | tebu expects a vector of length h*max(agg_order); nb=1 invariant satisfies this |
| Format | Return value: NAMED numeric vector (not matrix), with attr("FoReco") |
| Forbidden | Do NOT use FoReco::: triple-colon |

## IV. Step-by-Step Logic
1. Read current terml_g body.
2. Add guards at function START (before rml_g call):
```r
if (missing(base)) cli_abort("Argument {.arg base} is missing.", call = NULL)
base <- as.matrix(base)
if (!is.numeric(base)) cli_abort("{.arg base} must be numeric.", call = NULL)
if (ncol(obs) != 1L) cli_abort(
  "{.arg obs} for terml_g must have exactly 1 column (single bottom series).", call = NULL)
if (ncol(base) != ncol(hat)) cli_abort(
  "{.arg base} must have {ncol(hat)} columns; got {ncol(base)}.", call = NULL)
m <- max(agg_order)
if (nrow(base) %% m != 0) cli_abort(
  "{.arg base} must have rows divisible by max(agg_order) = {m}; got {nrow(base)}.",
  call = NULL)
```
3. After fitting, store ncol_hat:
```r
fit_obj$ncol_hat <- ncol(hat)
```
4. Replace return(fit_obj) with:
```r
base_features <- apply_norm_params(base, fit_obj$norm_params)
bts_vec <- predict(fit_obj, newdata = base_features)
# tebu expects a vector of length h_hf (h * max(agg_order))
reco_vec <- FoReco::tebu(bts_vec, agg_order = fit_obj$agg_order)
attr(reco_vec, "FoReco") <- new_foreco_info(list(
  fit = fit_obj, framework = "Temporal",
  forecast_horizon = nrow(base) / m,
  rfun = "terml_g", ml = approach))
reco_vec
```
5. Update @return: "named numeric vector matching FoReco::tebu output, length h × kt, names 'k-<order> h-<horizon>', with attr('FoReco')."
6. R CMD INSTALL --preclean . — clean install.
7. Smoke test:
```bash
Rscript -e '
library(FoRecoML); set.seed(1)
agg_order <- c(4L, 2L, 1L); m <- 4L; kt <- 7L; T_obs <- 60L; h <- 2L
hat <- matrix(rnorm(T_obs*kt), T_obs, kt); colnames(hat) <- paste0("L", 1:kt)
obs <- matrix(rnorm(T_obs*1), T_obs, 1); colnames(obs) <- "S1"
base <- matrix(rnorm(h*m*kt), h*m, kt); colnames(base) <- paste0("L", 1:kt)
r <- terml_g(base=base, hat=hat, obs=obs, agg_order=agg_order, seed=1L)
stopifnot(is.numeric(r), !is.null(names(r)), !is.null(attr(r, "FoReco")))
cat("OK length=", length(r), "\n")
'
```
8. Commit: `fix(rml_g): terml_g — restore fit+predict+tebu pipeline; return named vector (ak9.3)`

## V. Output Schema (Strict)
```toon
task_id: FoRecoML-ak9.3
success: bool
data:
  files_changed: [R/rml_g.R]
  function: terml_g
  smoke_test:
    is_numeric: bool
    has_names: bool
    has_foreco_attr: bool
    length: <int>
error_log: null | msg
```

## VI. Definition of Done
- [ ] terml_g body uses pipeline
- [ ] All 5 guards (missing, numeric, ncol_obs, ncol_base, nrow_mod)
- [ ] fit_obj$ncol_hat set
- [ ] bare new_foreco_info
- [ ] @return block updated to named vector
- [ ] Smoke test passes
- [ ] Conventional commit
close_reason: terml_g pipeline restored; smoke test passes (length 14 = h*kt); guards at entry; bare new_foreco_info via FoReco import

title: A1: csrml_g — fit + predict + csbu + return reconciled matrix
description:
# ak9.2: csrml_g — fit + apply_norm_params + predict + csbu + return matrix

## I. Context & Objective
- **Objective:** Replace csrml_g's bare `return(fit_obj)` with the fit+predict+reconcile pipeline so it returns a reconciled matrix with attr("FoReco").
- **Why:** Closes the core bug. Users call `csrml_g(base, hat, obs, agg_mat)` and expect reconciled output (per vignette and NEWS).
- **Reference:** docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-15-rml-g-reconcile-api-design.md §csrml_g pipeline.

## II. Input Specification
- **File:** R/rml_g.R, function csrml_g (lines 650–689 approx; check current state)
- **Format:** modify function body in-place; do NOT change signature

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
|------|-------|
| Logic | ncol(base) MUST equal ncol(hat). Verify before fit. |
| Logic | Use bare `new_foreco_info(...)` NOT `FoReco:::new_foreco_info` (NAMESPACE import handles visibility) |
| Logic | Reshape: matrix(bts_vec, nrow=h, ncol=nb) where h=nrow(base), nb=length(fit_obj$series_id_levels) |
| Logic | csbu input: h×nb (NOT nb×h — verified via `Rscript -e 'library(FoReco); args(csbu)'`) |
| Format | Return value: matrix with attr("FoReco") of class "foreco_info" |
| Boundary | Do NOT modify fitting code (rml_g call). Do NOT modify predict.rml_g_fit. |
| Forbidden | Do NOT use FoReco::: triple-colon. Do NOT silently swallow errors. |

## IV. Step-by-Step Logic
1. Read R/rml_g.R lines 650-695 to find current csrml_g body (post-revert state — returns fit_obj).
2. Add validation guard at function START (after normalize block, before rml_g call):
```r
if (missing(base)) cli_abort("Argument {.arg base} is missing, with no default.", call = NULL)
base <- as.matrix(base)
if (!is.numeric(base)) cli_abort("{.arg base} must be numeric.", call = NULL)
if (ncol(base) != ncol(hat)) cli_abort(
  "{.arg base} must have {ncol(hat)} columns (matching {.arg hat}); got {ncol(base)}.",
  call = NULL)
```
3. After fitting code, store `ncol_hat` on fit_obj:
```r
fit_obj$ncol_hat <- ncol(hat)
```
4. Replace `return(fit_obj)` with pipeline:
```r
base_features <- apply_norm_params(base, fit_obj$norm_params)
bts_vec <- predict(fit_obj, newdata = base_features)
h  <- nrow(base)
nb <- length(fit_obj$series_id_levels)
bts_mat  <- matrix(bts_vec, nrow = h, ncol = nb)
reco_mat <- FoReco::csbu(bts_mat, agg_mat = fit_obj$agg_mat)
attr(reco_mat, "FoReco") <- new_foreco_info(list(
  fit = fit_obj, framework = "Cross-sectional",
  forecast_horizon = h, rfun = "csrml_g", ml = approach))
reco_mat
```
5. Update roxygen @return block: was "rml_g_fit object with additional fields..." → "numeric matrix with attr('FoReco') of class 'foreco_info'."
6. Verify: `R CMD INSTALL --preclean . 2>&1 | tail -3` succeeds.
7. Smoke test:
```bash
Rscript -e '
library(FoRecoML); set.seed(1)
agg_mat <- t(c(1,1)); dimnames(agg_mat) <- list("A", c("B","C"))
n <- 3; T_obs <- 50; h <- 2
hat <- matrix(rnorm(T_obs*n), T_obs, n); colnames(hat) <- c("A","B","C")
obs <- matrix(rnorm(T_obs*2), T_obs, 2); colnames(obs) <- c("B","C")
base <- matrix(rnorm(h*n), h, n); colnames(base) <- c("A","B","C")
r <- csrml_g(base=base, hat=hat, obs=obs, agg_mat=agg_mat, seed=1L)
stopifnot(is.matrix(r), !is.null(attr(r, "FoReco")))
cat("OK dim=", dim(r), "\n")
'
```
8. Commit: `fix(rml_g): csrml_g — restore fit+predict+csbu pipeline; return reconciled matrix (ak9.2)`

## V. Output Schema (Strict)
```toon
task_id: FoRecoML-ak9.2
success: bool
data:
  files_changed: [R/rml_g.R]
  function: csrml_g
  smoke_test:
    is_matrix: bool
    has_foreco_attr: bool
    dim: [rows, cols]
error_log: null | msg
```

## VI. Definition of Done
- [ ] csrml_g body uses pipeline (no bare return(fit_obj))
- [ ] Validation guards in place (missing/numeric/ncol)
- [ ] fit_obj$ncol_hat set
- [ ] new_foreco_info bare call
- [ ] @return block updated
- [ ] R CMD INSTALL clean

- [ ] Conventional commit

title: verify predict.rml_g_fit: confirm raw-vector output is correct for wrapper-owned reconciliation (Arch B)
description:

predict.rml_g_fit stays a pure backend predictor returning a raw numeric vector.
Wrappers (ak9.2/3/4) call predict internally then apply csbu/tebu/ctbu.
No code changes to predict.rml_g_fit unless a bug is found during this verification.

1. Confirm `predict.rml_g_fit` with `series_id=NULL` and `newdata = t(base)` (h×n) produces a vector of length `h * nb` in series-major order: `[s1_h1..hh, s2_h1..hh, ..., snb_h1..hh]`.
2. Verify broadcast logic in rml_g.R:~972: `rep(object$series_id_levels, each = NROW(newdata))` — confirms series-major ordering, so `matrix(bts_vec, nrow=h, ncol=nb)` fills correctly column-major.
3. Confirm `object$series_id_levels` contains exactly the nb bottom-level series identifiers (not all n).
4. If any of the above fail: file a bug sub-ticket and DO NOT proceed to ak9.2/3/4.

## What NOT to change
- Do NOT add framework dispatch to predict.rml_g_fit.
- Do NOT change predict.rml_g_fit signature.
- Do NOT add csbu/tebu/ctbu calls inside predict.rml_g_fit.

## DoD
- Written comment on this ticket: "predict.rml_g_fit with series_id=NULL and h×n newdata returns h*nb vector in series-major order. VERIFIED." or a filed sub-ticket.
- No code changes unless bug found.

title: epic(rml_g): restore *_g fit+predict+reconcile API + rebuild cleanup
description:
# Epic: Restore *_g fit+predict+reconcile API

## Goal
Make csrml_g/terml_g/ctrml_g produce reconciled forecasts (not bare rml_g_fit) in a single call. Fix the regression where `base` was silently ignored.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] csrml_g returns reconciled matrix (h × n via csbu)
- [ ] terml_g returns named numeric vector (length h × kt via tebu)
- [ ] ctrml_g returns reconciled matrix (n × (h × kt) via ctbu)
- [ ] All three carry attr("FoReco") of class foreco_info
- [ ] devtools::test() passes; R CMD check --as-cran clean
- [ ] coherency check: agg_mat %*% bottom == top, tol = 1e-10
- [ ] NEWS.md + vignette aligned with new return type
- [ ] extract_reconciled_ml() dispatches correctly for new return type

## Context & Background
Design doc: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-15-rml-g-reconcile-api-design.md (committed 2ccb1a5).

Architecture B: wrappers own reconciliation. predict.rml_g_fit stays raw-prediction utility.

Key invariant: hat and base share column space (ncol(base) == ncol(hat)). This matches per-series csrml/terml/ctrml semantics. The rebuild's previous `ncol_hat ≠ n` fixture was buggy; fixtures rewritten per framework.

Per-framework return shapes:
- csrml_g → matrix (FoReco::csbu output)
- terml_g → named numeric vector length h*kt (FoReco::tebu output)
- ctrml_g → matrix n × (h*kt) where kt = sum(max(agg_order)/agg_order) (FoReco::ctbu output)

Reviewed via 3-iteration design-review-gate (PM/Architect/Designer/Security/CTO). Final iter: PM+Security APPROVED; Architect/Designer/CTO blockers fixed inline in commit 2ccb1a5.

## Sub-Agent Strategy
Sequential execution by hermetic ticket. Dependency chain:
- Wave 0 (done): ak9.1, ak9.9
- Wave 1: ak9.12 (fixture rewrite — precondition)
- Wave 2 (parallel): ak9.2, ak9.3, ak9.4, ak9.5, ak9.6, ak9.11
- Wave 3: ak9.10, ak9.7, ak9.13

Global guards:
- No `FoReco:::` triple-colon — use bare `new_foreco_info()`
- No fabricated APIs — every FoReco call signature verified live
- No silent failures — cli_abort on shape mismatch
- TDD: failing test before implementation per ticket
- Per CLAUDE.md: conventional commits, no AI attribution, no --no-verify
close_reason: Epic complete. *_g fit+predict+reconcile API restored. 10 child tickets landed. devtools::test FAIL 0 PASS 253.

title: fix(rml_g): restore fit+predict+reconcile pipeline in csrml_g/terml_g/ctrml_g
description:
## Root cause

All three *_g wrappers (csrml_g, terml_g, ctrml_g) in the rebuild accept base but silently ignore it. They return rml_g_fit instead of a reconciled forecast matrix. predict.rml_g_fit returns a raw numeric vector with no csbu/tebu/ctbu applied — so there is currently NO path in the rebuild to get reconciled forecasts from global ML.

## Old behaviour (ORIG_HEAD, correct)

ctrml_g(base, hat, obs, ...) →
  1. fit global model on hat/obs
  2. build Xtest_stack from base + norm_params
  3. predict → bts_raw
  4. ctbu(bts_raw, agg_mat, agg_order, ...) → reconciled matrix
  5. attach attr(reco_mat, "FoReco") and return

Same pattern: csrml_g → csbu, terml_g → tebu.

## New behaviour (rebuild, broken)

ctrml_g(base, hat, obs, ...) → rml_g_fit   # base never touched
predict.rml_g_fit(fit, newdata) → numeric vector  # no ctbu/csbu/tebu

## Fix required

1. After fitting, when base is provided: call internal predict path + apply framework-specific bottom-up reconciler (csbu / tebu / ctbu).
2. Return reconciled matrix with attr(reco_mat, "FoReco") matching old format.
3. predict.rml_g_fit can stay as raw-prediction utility — wrappers use it internally.

## Secondary findings (same audit)

- mlr3tuning + paradox still in DESCRIPTION Imports but tuning= param was dropped from all *_g functions in rebuild. Both are heavy unused dependencies — remove from Imports or restore tuning.
- sntz, round, tew dropped from *_g signatures. Verify intentional (no ticket needed if yes).
- normalize changed from logical to character enum ("none"/"zscore"/"robust") — breaking but intentional redesign; just needs NEWS entry.
- scale_estimator → scale_fn rename — same.

## Scope

R/rml_g.R: csrml_g, terml_g, ctrml_g bodies + predict.rml_g_fit (read-only reference)
May need FoReco::csbu / FoReco::tebu / FoReco::ctbu — verify available in current FoReco dep version.
close_reason: Superseded by epic FoRecoML-ak9; split into atomic tickets ak9.1–ak9.8.

title: C.1: fix terml mfh — delete vec2hmat, route via spd.13 path
description:
# C.1: fix terml mfh — delete vec2hmat, route via spd.13 path

## I. Context & Objective
Terml mfh wrappers stop pre-materializing hat/base; pass `h=h_hat`, `n=1L`, `kset=kset` to rml() so spd.13 mat2hmat_partial_from_sorted path handles per-iter expansion. BLOCKING math bug: pre-materialization + kset-only call → FoReco2matrix misinterprets matrix as cross-temporal → wrong shape, dim mismatch with y, or silent wrong fit if N==m by accident. Reference: R/terml.R lines 217, 319, 468-469 (vec2hmat call sites); R/reco_ml.R rml() pre-loop hoist (post-spd.14).

## II. Input Specification
- R/terml.R: 3 mfh sites (lines ~217, ~319, ~468-469)
- R/reco_ml.R: rml() already supports h, n formals (from spd.13/spd.14) — READ ONLY
- tests/testthat/test-terml-mfh.R: NEW FILE

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
|------|-------|
| Logic | DELETE vec2hmat call at all 3 sites. DELETE wrapper-side NA block (rep(sel_mat,NCOL(hat)) corruption risk). Compute kt_eff=sum(max(kset)/kset); total_cols=kt_eff. ADD keep_cols=sel_mat_keep_cols(sel_mat,total_cols) (non-NULL) to force T5 branch. BEFORE rml() call: hat<-matrix(hat,nrow=1L). Predict path: remove/fix expected_base_ncol guard (NCOL(base)=h*kt≠kt aborts incorrectly). |
| Format | Surgical edits to 3 sites only. No new utils. |
| Boundary | Only R/terml.R + new test file. csrml, ctrml, reco_ml, utils, FoReco UNTOUCHED. |
| Audit | Baseline = hand-verified small fixture (n=1, kt=4, p=3) since current terml mfh crashes; OR byte-equivalence vs vec2hmat-based reconstruction. vec2hmat(vec,h_hat,kset) produces h_hat×kt; mat2hmat(matrix(vec,nrow=1),h_hat,kset,n=1) produces SAME index pattern (n=1 outer rep is identity). |

## IV. Step-by-step Logic
1. `grep -n 'vec2hmat' R/terml.R` → confirm 3 sites at ~217, ~319, ~469.
2. At each site: DELETE `hat <- vec2hmat(vec=hat, h=h_hat, kset=kset)`; DELETE subsequent wrapper-side `na_col_mask(hat)` + `sel_mat[na_local] <- 0` block; compute `kt_eff <- sum(max(kset)/kset)`; `total_cols <- kt_eff`; `features_size <- total_cols`; ADD `keep_cols <- sel_mat_keep_cols(sel_mat, total_cols)` (non-NULL); ADD `hat <- matrix(hat, nrow=1L)` before rml() call.
3. Predict path (~line 319-329): remove or fix `expected_base_ncol` guard — replace with `NCOL(base) %% kt_eff == 0` shape check OR delete for mfh path.
4. At each rml() call: add `h=h_hat, n=1L, keep_cols=keep_cols` to args (kset already passed).
5. Add `tests/testthat/test-terml-mfh.R`: terml×mfh-all×lightgbm (no crash, bts dim correct); terml×mfh-hfts×lightgbm; terml×mfh-str×lightgbm; terml_fit+terml(fit=,base=) with mfh-all round-trip.
6. Run `Rscript -e 'devtools::test()'`; all 261 baseline + new tests pass.
7. Single commit: `fix(terml): defer vec2hmat into loop_body via h=h_hat, n=1L for mfh path`.

## V. Output Schema
```
sites_modified: [R/terml.R: ~217 (training), ~319 (predict base), ~468 (terml_fit training)]
tests_added: tests/testthat/test-terml-mfh.R (≥4 test_that blocks)
test_count_pre: 261
test_count_post: >=265
commit_count: 1
```

## VI. DoD
- [ ] `grep 'vec2hmat' R/terml.R` → 0 matches (excluding function definition)
- [ ] terml mfh tests all pass; output shape sanity-checked
- [ ] All baseline 261 tests still pass
- [ ] mw3.3 invariant intact: reco_ml.R `if (is.null(fit)) list(bts, fit, na_mask) else list(bts)` UNCHANGED
- [ ] spd.12+13+14 equivalence byte-identical (max_abs_diff == 0)
- [ ] Single commit; conventional commits; no AI attribution
close_reason: Merged. 271 tests pass. terml mfh now routes via mat2hmat_partial_from_sorted (spd.13 path). vec2hmat deleted from all 3 sites. Crash + silent-wrong-fit eliminated.

title: C.1: fix terml mfh — delete vec2hmat, route via spd.13 path
description:
# C.1: Fix terml mfh — delete vec2hmat, route via spd.13 path

## I. Objective
terml mfh wrappers stop pre-materializing hat/base; pass h=h_hat, n=1L, keep_cols=seq_len(kt_eff), kset=kset to rml().

## II. Input

3. Bypass expected_base_ncol check at line ~326 for mfh

title: A.1: fix unbound sel_mat in ctrml mfh-str* (6 sites)
description:
# A.1: Replace unbound sel_mat with strc_mat in ctrml mfh-str* branches

## I. Context & Objective
- Objective: Fix runtime crash in ctrml() and ctrml_fit() when features in {mfh-str, mfh-str-hfbts, mfh-str-bts}.
- Why: Pre-existing upstream bug; sel_mat referenced before assignment.
- Reference: ctrml.R sites 338, 341, 347 (training); 627, 630, 636 (ctrml_fit). terml.R:228 reference pattern. strc_mat defined at ctrml.R:279 and 571 in scope.

## II. Input Specification
- R/ctrml.R (6 sites)
- R/terml.R (reference only — UNTOUCHED)

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
|------|-------|
| Logic | RHS (sel_mat != 0) -> (strc_mat != 0). LHS sel_mat <- 1 * ... unchanged. |
| Format | 6 surgical line edits. No structural refactoring. |
| Boundary | Only ctrml.R + new test file. terml + csrml + reco_ml + utils UNTOUCHED. |
| Tone | Conventional commit. Body explains pre-existing upstream bug. |

## IV. Step-by-Step Logic
1. grep -n "sel_mat <- 1 \\* (sel_mat != 0)" R/ctrml.R -> 6 sites (3 ctrml, 3 ctrml_fit). NOTE: grep "sel_mat != 0" also matches 4 LEGITIMATE LHS-index lines `sel_mat[sel_mat != 0] <- 1` at 344, 351, 633, 640 — those are NOT the bug.
2. At each of 6 sites, replace ONLY RHS (sel_mat != 0) with (strc_mat != 0). Preserve LHS form.
3. Verify strc_mat in scope at each site.
4. Add tests/testthat/test-ctrml-mfh-str.R covering 3 mfh-str* features × lightgbm + ctrml_fit + ctrml(fit=) round-trip.
5. Run devtools::test() -> 215 baseline + new tests pass.
6. Single commit: fix(ctrml): replace unbound sel_mat with strc_mat in mfh-str branches.

## V. Output Schema
sites_fixed: [ctrml.R:338, ctrml.R:341, ctrml.R:347, ctrml.R:627, ctrml.R:630, ctrml.R:636]
tests_added: tests/testthat/test-ctrml-mfh-str.R
test_count_pre: 215
test_count_post: >= 219
commit_count: 1

## VI. Definition of Done
- [ ] grep "sel_mat <- 1 \\* (sel_mat != 0)" R/ctrml.R -> 0 matches
- [ ] grep "sel_mat <- 1 \\* (strc_mat != 0)" R/ctrml.R -> 6 matches
- [ ] grep "sel_mat\\[sel_mat != 0\\] <- 1" R/ctrml.R -> 4 matches (legitimate, untouched)
- [ ] All baseline + new mfh-str tests pass
- [ ] mw3.3, spd.12, spd.13 invariants intact
- [ ] Single commit; conventional commits; no AI attribution
close_reason: Merged at A.1 merge commit. 233 tests pass. 6 sites in R/ctrml.R fixed: sel_mat -> strc_mat in mfh-str/mfh-str-hfbts/mfh-str-bts branches at training (338,341,347) + ctrml_fit (627,630,636). New test-ctrml-mfh-str.R covers all 3 features + round-trip.

title: Epic A: ctrml mfh-str* correctness fixes (sel_mat unbound)
description:
# Epic A: ctrml mfh-str* correctness fixes (CRASH-CLASS)

## Goal
Fix runtime crash when user calls ctrml(features = "mfh-str" | "mfh-str-hfbts" | "mfh-str-bts").

## Success Criteria
- [ ] ctrml() + ctrml_fit() execute without "object sel_mat not found" error for all 3 mfh-str* features
- [ ] Numerical equivalence vs terml mfh-str pattern (sel_mat = 1 * (strc_mat != 0))
- [ ] testthat tests cover all 3 mfh-str* feature modes through ctrml end-to-end
- [ ] No regression on existing 215 tests

## Context & Background
Root cause: R/ctrml.R lines 337-352 (training) + 626-636 (ctrml_fit) initialize sel_mat from (sel_mat != 0) — but sel_mat is never assigned earlier. terml.R:228 correctly uses (strc_mat != 0). ctrml has strc_mat <- tmp$strc_mat at 279 and 571 in scope.

Pre-existing in upstream danigiro/FoRecoML (verified via git show origin/main:R/ctrml.R). Surfaced by spd.13 mfh test additions. P0 because mfh-str* are documented features.

## Sub-Agent Strategy
Single-task epic. One atomic implementer + spec/quality review. Surgical 6-line replacement + tests.

## Reference
Plan: .beads/plans/followups-plan.md (Epic A section)

title: epic(obs_mask): structural-zero row filtering in .stack_series()
description:
# Epic: obs_mask — Structural Zero Row Filtering

## Problem
.stack_series() flattens all obs into one target vector. Series with all-zero (or all-NA) obs across training time produce constant-target rows → catboost hard-errors; LightGBM/XGBoost train degenerate zero predictors.

## Fix
Accept optional `obs_mask` argument (logical matrix T_obs × nb, TRUE = valid observation). Exclude masked rows from X_train/y_train but preserve series_id levels (masked series still get predictions via neighbors at predict time).

## Default behavior
`obs_mask = NULL` → NO filtering. User must opt in explicitly.
`obs_mask = "auto"` → auto-detect rows where obs == 0 (opt-in, dangerous for valid zero-demand).

## Scope
- .stack_series(): filter train_idx by obs_mask
- All rml_g.* backends: pass obs_mask through to .stack_series()
- csrml_g/terml_g/ctrml_g: expose obs_mask param
- compute_rec_residuals: masked validation rows → NA in residual matrix (not excluded, not zero)

## Success Criteria
- catboost no longer hard-errors on hierarchies with structural zeros
- obs_mask=NULL preserves current behavior exactly
- Validation residuals: masked rows are NA, not included with fake zeros

## Sub-Agent Strategy
T1: .stack_series() filter logic
T2: backend passthrough + rml_g signature
T3: wrapper params (csrml_g/terml_g/ctrml_g)
T4: compute_rec_residuals NA handling
T5: tests + docs

title: T2: wrappers — terml_g/ctrml_g level_id param + csrml_g cli_abort
description:
# T2: Wrapper signatures + csrml_g rejection

## I. Context & Objective
- **Objective:** Add `level_id = FALSE` param to terml_g and ctrml_g. Pass `kset` (derived from agg_order) down to .stack_series(). csrml_g rejects level_id=TRUE.
- **Why:** Public API surface for level_id feature.

## II. Input Specification
- **File:** R/rml_g.R
- **Functions:**
  - csrml_g (lines ~655)
  - terml_g (lines ~775)
  - ctrml_g (lines ~870)

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
|------|-------|
| Logic | Pass `kset` to .stack_series() derived from `agg_order` (kset == agg_order in FoReco convention) |
| Logic | csrml_g: cli_abort if level_id != FALSE with clear actionable message |
| Format | Default FALSE; opt-in |
| Boundary | Do NOT touch rml_g() internals; only the 3 wrappers |
| Forbidden | Do NOT change other params or behavior when level_id=FALSE |

## IV. Step-by-Step Logic
1. csrml_g: add `level_id = FALSE`. At function start (after match.arg block):
   ```r
   if (isTRUE(level_id)) {
     cli_abort(
       "{.arg level_id} is not applicable to {.fn csrml_g}; cs hierarchy has no temporal axis.",
       call = NULL)
   }
   ```
2. terml_g: add `level_id = FALSE`. Pass to rml_g call (which threads to .stack_series): `rml_g(..., level_id = level_id, kset = agg_order, ...)`. Verify rml_g signature carries `level_id`/`kset` through (T1 should have arranged this).
3. ctrml_g: same as terml_g.
4. Update `@param level_id` roxygen block on terml_g and ctrml_g (NOT csrml_g — only document the abort there).
5. R CMD INSTALL --preclean .
6. Smoke test:
   ```r
   set.seed(1)
   agg_order <- c(4L, 2L, 1L); kt <- 7L; T_obs <- 60L; h <- 2L
   hat <- matrix(rnorm(T_obs * kt), T_obs, kt, dimnames=list(NULL, paste0("L",1:kt)))
   obs <- matrix(rnorm(T_obs), T_obs, 1, dimnames=list(NULL, "S1"))
   base <- matrix(rnorm(h * 4 * kt), h * 4, kt, dimnames=list(NULL, paste0("L",1:kt)))
   r_off <- terml_g(base=base, hat=hat, obs=obs, agg_order=agg_order, level_id=FALSE, seed=1L)
   r_on  <- terml_g(base=base, hat=hat, obs=obs, agg_order=agg_order, level_id=TRUE, seed=1L)
   stopifnot(is.numeric(r_off), is.numeric(r_on))
   # csrml_g abort
   agg_mat <- t(c(1,1)); dimnames(agg_mat) <- list("A", c("B","C"))
   hat_cs <- matrix(rnorm(150), 50, 3, dimnames=list(NULL, c("A","B","C")))
   obs_cs <- matrix(rnorm(100), 50, 2, dimnames=list(NULL, c("B","C")))
   base_cs <- matrix(rnorm(6), 2, 3, dimnames=list(NULL, c("A","B","C")))
   stopifnot(inherits(try(csrml_g(base=base_cs, hat=hat_cs, obs=obs_cs, agg_mat=agg_mat, level_id=TRUE, seed=1L), silent=TRUE), "try-error"))
   ```
7. Commit: `feat(rml_g): wrappers — level_id param on terml_g/ctrml_g; csrml_g cli_abort (T2)`

## V. Output Schema (Strict)
```toon
task_id: T2
success: bool
data:
  files_changed: [R/rml_g.R]
  smoke_test:
    terml_g_level_id_off: pass
    terml_g_level_id_on: pass
    csrml_g_abort: pass
error_log: null | msg
```

## VI. Definition of Done
- [ ] csrml_g rejects level_id=TRUE with helpful message
- [ ] terml_g/ctrml_g accept level_id and pass kset through
- [ ] roxygen @param updated on terml_g/ctrml_g
- [ ] R CMD INSTALL clean
- [ ] Smoke tests pass
- [ ] Conventional commit
close_reason: level_id param on terml_g/ctrml_g + csrml_g abort; predict.rml_g_fit reconstruction added; FAIL 0 PASS 253

title: T1: .stack_series() — wire kset + level_id derivation + post-encoding ncol_hat
description:
# T1: .stack_series() level_id wiring

## I. Context & Objective
- **Objective:** Add `level_id` parameter to `.stack_series()` (R/rml_g.R, currently lines ~20-90). When `level_id=TRUE`, append a per-row level_id integer column to X_stacked. Also: update `fit_obj$ncol_hat` to come from the **stacked** X width, not raw `ncol(hat)`.
- **Why:** Core data plumbing change underlying P0.
- **Reference:** prioritized recs §3 + T0 verification result.

## II. Input Specification
- **File:** R/rml_g.R
- **Targets:**
  - `.stack_series()` — line ~20-90; add `level_id = FALSE, kset = NULL` params
  - Backend `rml_g.lightgbm/xgboost/ranger/mlr3/catboost` ncol_hat assignment sites (lines 205, 271, 318, 359, 586, 1048) — must read from stacked X width when level_id=TRUE

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
|------|-------|
| Logic | Use level_id rule confirmed in T0 |
| Logic | Ordered integer, NOT one-hot |
| Logic | When kset is NULL but level_id=TRUE → cli_abort("level_id=TRUE requires kset") |
| Format | Add single column at the rightmost position of X_stacked, BEFORE series_id col |
| Boundary | Do NOT change behavior when level_id=FALSE (existing tests must still pass) |
| Forbidden | Do NOT touch predict.rml_g_fit (it already enforces ncol_hat) |

## IV. Step-by-Step Logic
1. Add `level_id = FALSE` and `kset = NULL` params to `.stack_series()` signature.
2. If `level_id=TRUE`:
   a. Require `kset` non-NULL (abort otherwise).
   b. Compute per-column level mapping from `kset` and `ncol(hat)`. Per T0's rule: `level_map <- rank(match(kset_per_col, sort(kset)))` where `kset_per_col` is the kset value for each hat column (derived from FoReco2matrix layout).
   c. Append `level_id` column to X_stacked: each row's level_id = level_map[col_of_origin_in_hat].
3. Return updated `X_stacked` from `.stack_series()`.
4. In each backend's `rml_g.*` method (lines 205, 271, 318, 359, 586, 1048): set `ncol_hat = ncol(X_stacked) - 1L` (excluding series_id). Make this the new universal rule regardless of level_id flag.
5. `R CMD INSTALL --preclean . 2>&1 | tail -3` — verify clean.
6. Quick smoke test:
   ```r
   library(FoRecoML)
   set.seed(1)
   hat <- matrix(rnorm(7 * 50), 50, 7)
   obs <- matrix(rnorm(50), 50, 1)
   s_off <- FoRecoML:::.stack_series(hat, obs, level_id = FALSE)
   s_on  <- FoRecoML:::.stack_series(hat, obs, level_id = TRUE, kset = c(4, 2, 1))
   stopifnot(ncol(s_on$X_stacked) == ncol(s_off$X_stacked) + 1L)
   ```
7. Commit: `feat(rml_g): wire kset + level_id into .stack_series(); ncol_hat from stacked width (T1)`

## V. Output Schema (Strict)
```toon
task_id: T1
success: bool
data:
  files_changed: [R/rml_g.R]
  smoke_test:
    ncol_diff_when_level_id: 1L
    install_clean: bool
error_log: null | msg
```

## VI. Definition of Done
- [ ] `.stack_series()` accepts level_id + kset
- [ ] X_stacked gains 1 column when level_id=TRUE
- [ ] ncol_hat read from stacked width in all 5 backends
- [ ] R CMD INSTALL clean
- [ ] Smoke test passes
- [ ] Conventional commit
close_reason: .stack_series() level_id wiring complete + quality fixes: ncol_hat overrides removed from wrappers; kset validation; doc updated; FAIL 0 PASS 253

title: T0: verify FoReco2matrix column layout convention (precondition)
description:
# T0: Precondition — Verify FoReco2matrix layout

## I. Context & Objective
- **Objective:** Read-only investigation: confirm how `FoReco::FoReco2matrix` orders columns relative to temporal aggregation levels. Pin the derivation rule for level_id BEFORE T1.
- **Why:** Without verified layout, T1's level_id mapping could silently invert.
- **Reference:** prioritized recs doc §4 NOTE; rml_g.R:24 (kset reserved).

## II. Input Specification
- No file edits.
- Output: comment on this ticket with verified rule + at least one fixture trace.

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
|------|-------|
| Logic | Use a concrete agg_order example, e.g., c(4,2,1), to trace |
| Format | Document via `bd comment` on this ticket |
| Forbidden | Do NOT edit R/ files in T0 |

## IV. Step-by-Step Logic
1. Run:
   ```bash
   Rscript -e '
     library(FoReco)
     set.seed(1)
     hat_long <- matrix(rnorm(7 * 50), 50, 7)  # 7 = sum(4/c(4,2,1))
     parts <- FoReco::FoReco2matrix(hat_long, kset = c(4, 2, 1))
     cat("kset = c(4, 2, 1)\n")
     for (i in seq_along(parts)) cat("parts[[", i, "]] ncol =", NCOL(parts[[i]]), "\n")
   '
   ```
2. Note: which list index corresponds to which k value? (Expected: parts[[1]] has 1 col [k=4 annual], parts[[2]] has 2 [k=2], parts[[3]] has 4 [k=1]).
3. Confirm the per-column level mapping for an h×kt hat matrix.
4. Document the level_id assignment rule with a concrete worked example.
5. If layout differs from assumption (level_id=1 → smallest k), update the derivation in T1's logic.

## V. Output Schema (Strict)
```toon
task_id: T0
success: bool
data:
  kset_example: [4, 2, 1]
  parts_ncols_observed: [N, N, N]
  level_id_rule: "level_id = rank(match(kset, sort(kset)))" | corrected_alternative
  worked_example: "<short trace>"
error_log: null | msg
```

## VI. Definition of Done
- [ ] FoReco2matrix tested with a real fixture
- [ ] level_id derivation rule documented in this ticket comment
- [ ] No file edits made
- [ ] Comment posted: `bd comment FoRecoML-a0t.X "<rule + trace>"`
close_reason: VERIFIED: FoReco2matrix parts[[1]]=k=4 (coarsest). level_id=rank(match(kset,sort(kset))): k=1→1 (finest), k=4→3 (coarsest). Derivation rule confirmed.

comments.author: Dennis Alexis Valin Dittrich
comments.text: VERIFIED: level_id derivation rule: level_id = rank(match(kset, sort(kset))). FoReco2matrix parts[[1]] = k=4 (COARSEST temporal granularity). Worked example: kset=c(4,2,1) → level_id values (k=4→3, k=2→2, k=1→1). Correctly encodes: level_id=1 (finest), level_id=max (coarsest).

title: epic(P0): level_id feature for global ML wrappers
description:
# Epic P0: level_id Feature for terml_g/ctrml_g

## Goal
Add `level_id` ordered-integer feature to `.stack_series()` so global ML models can learn level-specific correction magnitudes. Opt-in flag, backwards-compat default FALSE.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `terml_g(level_id=TRUE)` and `ctrml_g(level_id=TRUE)` add a level_id feature column to the stacked X
- [ ] `csrml_g(level_id=TRUE)` → cli_abort (not applicable)
- [ ] `fit_obj$ncol_hat` reflects post-encoding stacked width when level_id=TRUE
- [ ] LightGBM importance on synthetic level-effect DGP shows level_id > 5%
- [ ] Coherency invariant preserved: `agg_mat %*% bottom == top`, tol = 1e-10
- [ ] Existing post-Epic ak9 tests pass unchanged
- [ ] NEWS.md + roxygen + pkgdown article shipped together
- [ ] R CMD check --as-cran clean

## Context & Background
Design doc: `docs/research/2026-05-16-ctrml-g-recommendations-prioritized.md` (v2.1, gate-approved 5/5 iter 3).

Encoding: ordered integer `1=finest (smallest k) → max=coarsest (largest k)`. Derive via `rank(match(kset, sort(kset)))`. NOTE: this is REVERSE of `FoReco::FoReco2matrix` list index ordering.

Mechanism: wire `kset` (currently reserved+unused in `.stack_series()`, rml_g.R:24) through from terml_g/ctrml_g. Per-column level_id map → broadcast to per-row feature when level_id=TRUE.

Source paper: Yingjie & Abolghasemi (2024 arXiv:2411.06394) — cross-hierarchy features critical for global ML.

## Sub-Agent Strategy
Sequential execution:
- T0: precondition — verify FoReco2matrix layout convention (no code change)
- T1: implement .stack_series() level_id wiring
- T2: wrappers — signature param + csrml_g abort
- T3: docs (NEWS + roxygen + pkgdown article)
- T4-T7: tests (RED→GREEN→REFACTOR)

Global guards:
- Use `rank(match(kset, sort(kset)))` for level_id mapping; do NOT use list-index ordering
- ncol_hat MUST come from stacked X width, NOT raw ncol(hat)
- bare new_foreco_info() (no triple-colon)
- ordered integer, NOT one-hot
close_reason: All 8 child tickets closed. level_id feature complete. FAIL 0 PASS 268.

description:

| Format | testthat 3 style; use new fixture helpers from ak9.12 |
| Forbidden | Don't delete tests; rewrite them |

## IV. Step-by-Step Logic
1. **Update test-rml-g-wrappers.R:**
   - Line 26 (csrml_g test): `expect_s3_class(result, "rml_g_fit")` →
     `expect_true(is.matrix(result)); expect_false(is.null(attr(result, "FoReco")))`

     `attr(result, "FoReco")$fit$norm_params` (and similarly for any other field access)

# Regression tests for restored *_g reconcile pipeline
test_that("csrml_g returns reconciled matrix with FoReco attr + coherency", {
  skip_if_not_installed("lightgbm")
  fx <- make_g_fixture_cs()
  result <- csrml_g(base = fx$base, hat = fx$hat, obs = fx$obs,
                    agg_mat = fx$agg_mat, approach = "lightgbm", seed = 1L)

  expect_false(is.null(attr(result, "FoReco")))

title: ak9.4.5: extract_reconciled_ml — add dispatch branch for rml_g_fit via attr(FoReco)
description:
# ak9.10: extract_reconciled_ml — dispatch branch for rml_g_fit via attr(FoReco)

## I. Context & Objective
- **Objective:** Add early-return branch to extract_reconciled_ml() so that reco objects from *_g (matrix/vector with attr("FoReco")$fit of class rml_g_fit) return the fit.
- **Why:** Current function only handles inherits(reco, "rml_fit"); new *_g return type is opaque to it.

## II. Input Specification
- **File:** R/utils.R, function extract_reconciled_ml
- **Format:** insert near top of function (after argument validation, before existing rml_fit branch)

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
|------|-------|
| Logic | Only return fit when attr("FoReco")$fit inherits "rml_g_fit" |
| Boundary | Do NOT modify existing rml_fit branch (works for per-series csrml/terml/ctrml) |

## IV. Step-by-Step Logic
1. Read R/utils.R::extract_reconciled_ml current body.
2. Add at function start (before the existing `inherits(reco, "rml_fit")` check or in parallel):
```r
foreco_attr <- attr(reco, "FoReco")
if (!is.null(foreco_attr) && inherits(foreco_attr$fit, "rml_g_fit")) {
  return(foreco_attr$fit)
}
```
3. R CMD INSTALL --preclean . clean.
4. Smoke test using ak9.2 output:
```bash
Rscript -e '
library(FoRecoML); set.seed(1)
agg_mat <- t(c(1,1)); dimnames(agg_mat) <- list("A", c("B","C"))
hat <- matrix(rnorm(150), 50, 3); colnames(hat) <- c("A","B","C")
obs <- matrix(rnorm(100), 50, 2); colnames(obs) <- c("B","C")
base <- matrix(rnorm(6), 2, 3); colnames(base) <- c("A","B","C")
r <- csrml_g(base=base, hat=hat, obs=obs, agg_mat=agg_mat, seed=1L)
fit <- extract_reconciled_ml(r)
stopifnot(inherits(fit, "rml_g_fit"))
cat("OK\n")
'
```
5. Commit: `fix(utils): extract_reconciled_ml — dispatch for rml_g_fit via attr(FoReco) (ak9.10)`

## V. Output Schema
```toon
task_id: FoRecoML-ak9.10
success: bool
data:
  files_changed: [R/utils.R]
  smoke_test:
    returns_rml_g_fit: bool
error_log: null | msg
```

## VI. Definition of Done
- [ ] Dispatch branch added
- [ ] Existing rml_fit branch unmodified
- [ ] Smoke test passes
- [ ] Conventional commit
close_reason: extract_reconciled_ml dispatch landed; smoke test confirms returns rml_g_fit for *_g output

title: G.2 (spd.28): chunked incremental global training for *_g functions
description:
# G.2 (spd.28): Chunked Incremental Global Training for *_g Functions

Ticket: FoRecoML-1cn (P1)

## I. Context & Objective

**Problem (analyst diagnosis)**: ctrml_g stacks ALL p series at once before training.
For user's case (p=2432, T_obs=72, compact cols=15,597): stacked matrix =
175,104 × 15,597 × 8B = **21.8 GB** → OOM on 16 GB RAM before lgb.train() is called.

**Root cause asymmetry**:
- ctrml (per-series): N=72 rows/booster → LightGBM never engages num_threads internally.
  Parallelism must come from n_workers (cross-daemon), which adds serialization overhead.
- ctrml_g (global): num_threads=9 engages on 175K-row booster. BUT OOMs at user scale.

**Solution**: Train on batches of `batch_size` series at a time (e.g., 200 series →
14.4K rows, 1.8 GB), warm-starting each batch from the previous model via
`init_model` (lightgbm/xgboost) or equivalent (catboost). Final model sees all
data incrementally; num_threads engages on each batch.

**Out**: `batch_size` param added to `csrml_g/terml_g/ctrml_g`. Auto-computes
from available RAM. Incremental warm-start for lightgbm/xgboost/catboost.
ranger/mlr3 abort when stacked matrix exceeds RAM threshold (no warm-start API).

## II. Input Specification

- R/csrml.R: csrml_g() + csrml_g_fit() if needed
- R/terml.R: terml_g()
- R/ctrml.R: ctrml_g()
- R/reco_ml.R: train_incremental_global() new internal helper + rml_g.* update for nrounds splitting

## III. Constraints & Guards

| Type | Guard |
|------|-------|
| **Architecture** | Training in k batches of batch_size series each. Total nrounds split: each batch gets `ceiling(nrounds / k_batches)` rounds. Final model has ≈ nrounds trees total. lightgbm/xgboost/catboost only. |
| **batch_size="auto"** | `floor(0.2 × available_ram_bytes() / (T_obs × active_ncol × 8))`, clamped to [1, p]. T_obs = NROW(obs) / p (obs rows per series). |
| **Single batch (batch_size ≥ p)** | Reduces to current behavior. Output BYTE-IDENTICAL to unchunked ctrml_g (single call, no warm-start needed). |
| **Multi-batch** | Output is NOT byte-identical to unchunked (different data-presentation order in boosting). Test: RMSE within 1.05× of unchunked on same fixture. |
| **ranger/mlr3** | No warm-start API → cli_abort if `p × T_obs × active_ncol × 8 > 0.5 × available_ram_bytes()`. Document: use lightgbm/xgboost for large-scale global training. |
| **Normalization** | Per-series norm_params computed per-batch (each series appears in exactly one batch). Accumulated into a list of p params across batches. Returned in fit$norm_params. Predict-reuse reads existing fit$norm_params unchanged. |
| **nrounds splitting invariant** | `n_per_batch = ceiling(nrounds / n_batches)`. Total trees after k batches = k × n_per_batch ≈ nrounds. Document that multi-batch nrounds may slightly exceed target due to ceiling. |
| **Boundary** | Only R/csrml.R, R/terml.R, R/ctrml.R, R/reco_ml.R (new helper). rml_g.* methods untouched (warm-start lives in the wrappers, not dispatch methods). |
| **Audit** | Single-batch: byte-identical to current ctrml_g. Multi-batch: RMSE ≤1.05× of unchunked on small fixture. |

## IV. Step-by-Step Logic

### Step 1: New internal helper train_incremental_global() in R/reco_ml.R

```r
# Trains (or warm-starts) a global model on one batch of stacked data.
# Returns updated global_model.
# Only called when batch_size < p; otherwise rml_g() is called directly (no helper).
train_incremental_global <- function(approach, X_batch, y_batch, series_id_batch,
                                      global_model, n_rounds, params, seed) {
  class(approach) <- c(class(approach), approach)
  UseMethod("train_incremental_global", approach)
}

train_incremental_global.lightgbm <- function(approach, X_batch, y_batch,
                                               series_id_batch, global_model,
                                               n_rounds, params, seed) {
  X_mat <- cbind(X_batch, series_id = as.integer(series_id_batch))
  colnames(X_mat) <- c(paste0("f", seq_len(ncol(X_batch))), "series_id")
  # CRITICAL: free_raw_data = FALSE required for init_model warm-start.
  # free_raw_data=TRUE causes "cannot set predictor after free raw data" error.
  dset <- lgb.Dataset(X_mat, label = y_batch, free_raw_data = FALSE)
  lgb.train(params = params, data = dset, nrounds = n_rounds,
             init_model = global_model)
}

train_incremental_global.xgboost <- function(approach, X_batch, y_batch,
                                              series_id_batch, global_model,
                                              n_rounds, params, seed) {
  X_mat <- cbind(X_batch, series_id = as.integer(series_id_batch))
  colnames(X_mat) <- c(paste0("f", seq_len(ncol(X_batch))), "series_id")
  dm <- xgb.DMatrix(X_mat, label = y_batch)
  xgb.train(params = params, data = dm, nrounds = n_rounds,
             xgb_model = global_model)
}

train_incremental_global.catboost <- function(approach, X_batch, y_batch,
                                               series_id_batch, global_model,
                                               n_rounds, params, seed) {
  X_mat <- cbind(X_batch, series_id = as.integer(series_id_batch))
  colnames(X_mat) <- c(paste0("f", seq_len(ncol(X_batch))), "series_id")
  cat_idx <- ncol(X_mat) - 1L  # 0-based
  pool <- catboost::catboost.load_pool(X_mat, label = y_batch,
                                        cat_features = cat_idx)
  catboost::catboost.train(pool,
    params = modifyList(params, list(iterations = n_rounds)),
    init_model = global_model)
}

train_incremental_global.default <- function(approach, ...) {
  cli::cli_abort("Incremental global training not supported for this approach.")
}
```

### Step 2: Add batch_size param to ctrml_g/terml_g/csrml_g signatures

```r
ctrml_g <- function(..., batch_size = "auto", ...) { ... }
```

Add `@param batch_size Integer or "auto". Number of series per training batch.
"auto" computes from available RAM (target 20% per batch). Single batch (batch_size >= p)
is byte-identical to previous ctrml_g behavior. Multi-batch uses incremental warm-start;
only supported for lightgbm, xgboost, catboost.`

### Step 3: Chunked training logic in each *_g wrapper

Insert between feature-matrix construction and the rml_g() call:

```r
# After: X_list built (per-series feature matrices)
# Before: normalize_stack + rml_g call

# --- Resolve batch_size ---
if (identical(batch_size, "auto")) {
  # IMPORTANT: obs orientation varies per wrapper.
  # ctrml_g: obs enters as nb×N, is transposed internally → T_obs = NCOL(obs) = N*m
  # terml_g: obs is N_obs vector length nb*N → T_obs = length(obs)/nb
  # csrml_g: obs is N×nb → T_obs = NROW(obs)
  # Use NCOL(obs) for ctrml_g (original pre-transpose has N*m cols = training obs per series).
  # Each wrapper computes T_obs from its own local obs shape BEFORE any transpose.
  T_obs <- NCOL(obs)  # ctrml_g: orig obs is nb×(N*m), so NCOL = N*m = T_obs
  bytes_per_series <- T_obs * active_ncol * 8
  ram_budget <- 0.2 * available_ram_bytes()
  batch_size <- max(1L, min(nb, as.integer(ram_budget / bytes_per_series)))
}

# ranger/mlr3 OOM guard
if (approach %in% c("ranger", "mlr3")) {
  stacked_bytes <- nb * T_obs * active_ncol * 8
  if (stacked_bytes > 0.5 * available_ram_bytes()) {
    cli::cli_abort(c(
      "Stacked matrix ({format(stacked_bytes/1e9, digits=2)} GB) exceeds 50% of available RAM.",
      "i" = "Use approach = 'lightgbm' or 'xgboost' for large-scale global training.",
      "i" = "Alternatively, reduce features (features='compact') or p."
    ))
  }
}

if (batch_size >= nb) {
  # --- Single batch: current behavior ---
  X_stack <- do.call(rbind, X_list)
  y_stack <- as.vector(obs)
  series_id_vec <- rep(seq_len(nb), each = T_obs)
  norm_out <- normalize_stack(X_stack, series_id_vec, normalize, scale_estimator)
  g_result <- rml_g(approach, norm_out$X_norm, y_stack, series_id_vec,
                     Xtest_stack, fine_tune_rounds, fit, seed,
                     norm_params = norm_out$params, params = params)

} else {
  # --- Multi-batch incremental training ---
  batches <- split(seq_len(nb), ceiling(seq_len(nb) / batch_size))
  n_batches <- length(batches)
  n_per_batch <- ceiling(nrounds / n_batches)  # nrounds from params

  global_model <- NULL
  all_norm_params <- vector("list", nb)
  names(all_norm_params) <- as.character(seq_len(nb))

  cli::cli_inform("ctrml_g: incremental training over {n_batches} batches of {batch_size} series.")

  for (b_idx in seq_along(batches)) {
    batch_series <- batches[[b_idx]]
    X_batch <- do.call(rbind, X_list[batch_series])
    T_obs_vec <- rep(batch_series, each = T_obs)
    y_batch <- as.vector(obs[, batch_series, drop = FALSE])

    norm_out <- normalize_stack(X_batch, T_obs_vec, normalize, scale_estimator)
    for (s in batch_series) all_norm_params[[as.character(s)]] <- norm_out$params[[as.character(s)]]

    global_model <- train_incremental_global(
      approach, norm_out$X_norm, y_batch, T_obs_vec,
      global_model, n_per_batch, params_lgb_or_xgb, seed)
  }

  # Prediction (same as single-batch but uses accumulated global_model)
  Xtest_stack <- ...  # build Xtest_stack from base features
  g_result <- rml_g(approach, X_stack = NULL, y_stack = NULL, series_id_vec,
                     Xtest_stack, fine_tune_rounds = 0L,
                     fit = list(global_model = global_model, series_models = NULL,
                                norm_params = all_norm_params),
                     seed, norm_params = all_norm_params, params = params)
}
```

Note: `nrounds` must be extracted from `params` (e.g., `params$nrounds` for lightgbm) before the dispatch. The `train_incremental_global` methods receive `n_per_batch`, not the full nrounds, to avoid over-training.

### Step 3b: Chunked loop pseudocode for terml_g and csrml_g

**terml_g**: obs is a length-N*nb vector (temporal stacked). T_obs = length(obs) / nb.
```r
# terml_g batch_size resolution:
T_obs <- length(obs) / nb   # temporal obs per series
bytes_per_series <- T_obs * active_ncol * 8
# ...same batch loop as ctrml_g but feature engineering uses tetools/input2rtw...
```
Feature stacking: per-series X_i comes from temporal input2rtw on hat slice.
Post-reconciliation: tebu (same as existing terml).

**csrml_g**: obs is N×nb matrix. T_obs = NROW(obs).
```r
# csrml_g batch_size resolution:
T_obs <- NROW(obs)   # obs per series (cross-sectional)
bytes_per_series <- T_obs * active_ncol * 8
# ...same batch loop, feature engineering via direct hat column slicing...
```
Post-reconciliation: csbu (same as existing csrml).

Both follow the **exact same batch loop structure** as ctrml_g (Step 3): `split(seq_len(nb), ceiling(seq_len(nb) / batch_size))` → per-batch normalize + train_incremental_global + accumulate norm_params.

### Step 4: Tests (tests/testthat/test-g2-chunked.R)

1. **Single batch byte-identical** (lightgbm): `ctrml_g(batch_size=nb)` → `expect_equal(bts, bts_no_chunk, tolerance=0)`. withr::defer cache cleanup.

2. **Multi-batch RMSE quality**: small fixture (nb=4, T=20, m=4). `batch_size=2` (2 batches of 2 series). RMSE ratio ≤1.05 vs single-batch.
   ```r
   r_chunk <- ctrml_g(batch_size=2, approach="lightgbm", params=list(nrounds=20L, nthread=1L))
   r_full  <- ctrml_g(batch_size=nb, ...)
   rmse_chunk <- sqrt(mean((r_chunk$bts - truth)^2))
   rmse_full  <- sqrt(mean((r_full$bts  - truth)^2))
   expect_lte(rmse_chunk / rmse_full, 1.05)
   ```

3. **Auto batch_size < nb at tight RAM**: inject `.rml_cache$ram_bytes = 1L` → `batch_size` resolves to 1. withr::defer cleanup. `expect_no_error(ctrml_g(batch_size="auto", ...))`.

4. **ranger OOM abort**: `expect_error(ctrml_g(approach="ranger", batch_size="auto", ...), regexp="Stacked matrix.*exceeds 50%")`. Inject small RAM via cache_swap.

5. **Warm-start tree count** (lightgbm; requires lightgbm installed):
   ```r
   test_that("G.2: lightgbm warm-start accumulates trees across batches", {
     skip_if_not_installed("lightgbm"); skip_on_cran()
     # nb=4, batch_size=2 → 2 batches; nrounds=10 → 5 rounds/batch → ~10 total
     r <- ctrml_g(batch_size=2, params=list(nrounds=10L, nthread=1L), ...)
     n_trees <- r$fit[[1]]$global_model$num_trees()
     expect_gte(n_trees, 8L)   # at least ~80% of target nrounds
     expect_lte(n_trees, 12L)  # at most ~120% (ceiling rounding)
   })
   ```

### Step 5: devtools::document() + Run tests

All 459 baseline pass + 5+ new G.2 tests.

### Step 6: Commit

`perf(rml): chunked incremental global training for *_g (G.2 spd.28 FoRecoML-1cn)`

Body: mechanism, batch_size auto formula, supported backends, test strategy.

## V. Output Schema

```
files_modified: [R/reco_ml.R, R/ctrml.R, R/terml.R, R/csrml.R]
new_helper: train_incremental_global + methods (lightgbm/xgboost/catboost/default)
new_param: batch_size = "auto" in all 3 wrappers
test_file: tests/testthat/test-g2-chunked.R
test_count_pre: 459
test_count_post: >= 464
commit_count: 1
```

## VI. Definition of Done

- [ ] train_incremental_global S3 generic + 4 methods (lightgbm, xgboost, catboost, default) in reco_ml.R
- [ ] free_raw_data = FALSE in train_incremental_global.lightgbm (prevents init_model crash)
- [ ] batch_size = "auto" param in ctrml_g/terml_g/csrml_g with @param doc
- [ ] T_obs computed correctly per wrapper: NCOL(obs) for ctrml_g; length(obs)/nb for terml_g; NROW(obs) for csrml_g
- [ ] Auto batch_size: floor(0.2 × available_ram_bytes() / (T_obs × active_ncol × 8)) clamped [1, nb]
- [ ] ranger/mlr3 OOM guard: cli_abort when stacked > 0.5 × RAM
- [ ] Single-batch path byte-identical to pre-G.2 (test 1)
- [ ] Multi-batch path RMSE ≤1.05× of single-batch (test 2)
- [ ] norm_params accumulated across batches; fit$norm_params covers all p series
- [ ] nrounds splitting: ceiling(nrounds / k_batches) rounds per batch
- [ ] cli_inform message when multi-batch active (shows n_batches + batch_size)
- [ ] Warm-start tree count verified: n_trees ≈ nrounds (test 5, within ±20%)
- [ ] terml_g chunked loop implemented (T_obs=length(obs)/nb; tebu post-reconciliation)
- [ ] csrml_g chunked loop implemented (T_obs=NROW(obs); csbu post-reconciliation)
- [ ] 459 baseline tests unchanged; test_count_post ≥ 464
- [ ] Single commit; conventional commits; no AI attribution

## Risks

- **R1**: nrounds extraction from params differs per backend (lgb: params$nrounds; xgb: params$nrounds; catboost: params$iterations). Extract in wrapper before calling helper. The helper receives `n_per_batch` NOT the full params.
- **R2**: lgb.Dataset `free_raw_data = FALSE` required for `init_model` warm-start. `free_raw_data = TRUE` causes "cannot set predictor after free raw data" error on second batch. This is a VERIFIED bug — always use `free_raw_data = FALSE` in train_incremental_global.lightgbm.
- **R3**: ranger output is NOT byte-identical to unchunked even for single-batch (different internal RNG state). Document: ranger global training is deterministic only with fixed seed; the single-batch byte-identical test (test 1) is lightgbm-specific.
- **R4**: predict-reuse with multi-batch model: rml_g receives fit=existing (the global_model from incremental training). The predict path in rml_g.lightgbm etc. is unchanged — single model predicts on Xtest regardless of how many batches trained it.
close_reason: Merged. 466 tests pass. train_incremental_global S3 + 4 methods; batch_size='auto' in all 3 _g wrappers; free_raw_data=FALSE in lgb; catboost column contract fixed; @param batch_size added; NAMESPACE registered.
