title: B10: lazy NULL na_cols_list when no NAs filtered
description:
# B10: Lazy NULL na_cols_list when no NAs filtered

## I. Objective
After training loop in rml(), if NO series triggered NA-column filtering (i.e., every `out[[i]]$na_mask` is NULL), set `fit$na_cols_list <- NULL` instead of `list(NULL, NULL, ..., NULL)` (length p). Predict-time replay already handles NULL via existing back-compat guard (`!is.null(fit$na_cols_list)` at line ~177).

## II. Input
- R/reco_ml.R: post-loop fit assembly (line where `fit$na_cols_list <- lapply(out, ...)` is set)
- R/reco_ml.R: predict-reuse path NA replay (line ~177, already guards NULL)

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
|------|-------|
| Logic | Output unchanged. fit$na_cols_list is NULL iff no series had NAs; populated list iff at least one did. Predict replay back-compat preserves both paths. |
| Format | Single conditional after `na_cols_list <- lapply(...)`. |
| Boundary | Only R/reco_ml.R. |
| Audit | spd.12 NA-injection test verifies populated path. New test: train without NAs in hat → fit$na_cols_list is NULL. Round-trip predict still works. |

## IV. Step-by-step

1. Locate the assembly: `fit$na_cols_list <- lapply(out, "[[", "na_mask")` in R/reco_ml.R.
2. Replace with:
   ```r
   na_masks <- lapply(out, `[[`, "na_mask")
   if (all(vapply(na_masks, is.null, logical(1)))) {
     fit$na_cols_list <- NULL
   } else {
     fit$na_cols_list <- na_masks
   }
   ```
3. Verify predict-reuse `!is.null(fit$na_cols_list)` guard (line ~177) handles both cases:
   - NULL: skip NA replay (no NAs to apply)
   - non-NULL list: per-series replay
4. Add test in tests/testthat/test-spd12-predict-reuse-na.R (or new test):
   - Train ctrml on clean hat (no NAs) → `expect_null(mdl$na_cols_list)`
   - Train ctrml on NA-injected hat → `expect_type(mdl$na_cols_list, "list")` + at least one non-NULL entry
   - Round-trip predict in both cases.
5. Run `Rscript -e 'devtools::test()' 2>&1 | tail -5`. All 299+ pass.
6. Single commit: `perf(rml): lazy NULL na_cols_list when no NAs filtered (B10)`.

## V. Output Schema
sites_modified: R/reco_ml.R (post-loop assembly)
test_count_pre: 299
test_count_post: >= 301
commit_count: 1

## VI. DoD
- [ ] fit$na_cols_list is NULL when no NAs encountered
- [ ] fit$na_cols_list is populated list when ≥1 series had NAs
- [ ] Predict-reuse handles both states (NA fixture round-trips)
- [ ] No-NA fixture round-trips
- [ ] mw3.3 invariant intact
- [ ] spd.12+13+14 equivalence preserved
- [ ] Single commit
close_reason: Merged. 367 tests pass. B4: ranger default + ML-aware 0.48 threshold (ranger+RF) + ranger multiplier=5 in estimate_peak_bytes. B7: qs_nthreads_adaptive(). B8: dim()<- for in-place terml mfh. B10: lazy NULL na_cols_list. Epics H/I/J complete.

title: B9: drop redundant sel_mat from .args when global_id_list populated
description:
# B9: Drop sel_mat from .args when global_id_list populated

## I. Objective
After spd.15 precomputes global_id_list, the loop_body matrix-sel_mat branch reads global_id from the list instead of `which(sel_mat[, i] != 0)`. When global_id_list is non-NULL, sel_mat is shipped redundantly via mirai .args. Conditionally set `sel_mat = NULL` in .args to drop the redundancy.

## II. Input
- R/reco_ml.R: dispatch block (.args list + sequential call)
- R/reco_ml.R: loop_body (verify sel_mat is only read via global_id_list[[i]] when that's available)

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
|------|-------|
| Logic | Byte-identical output. Verify loop_body has NO sel_mat reference outside the `is.null(global_id_list)` branch — if found, must keep sel_mat or refactor that path first. |
| Format | Single conditional in .args block: `sel_mat = if (is.null(global_id_list)) sel_mat else NULL`. Same for sequential call. |
| Boundary | Only R/reco_ml.R. |
| Audit | spd.12+13+14 equivalence + mw3.3 + all baseline tests preserved. |

## IV. Step-by-step

1. **Audit first**: grep `sel_mat` in loop_body body. Confirm only used where global_id_list could not provide. Common sites:
   - `which(sel_mat[, i] != 0)` — when NCOL(sel_mat)>1, this is replaced by global_id_list[[i]] (spd.15). Safe to NULL sel_mat here.
   - scalar / sparseVector sel_mat path: still needs sel_mat. Don't NULL.
2. Add conditional in both dispatch sites (sequential ~line 308, mirai_map ~line 338):
   ```r
   sel_mat_arg <- if (!is.null(global_id_list)) NULL else sel_mat
   ```
   then `.args = list(..., sel_mat = sel_mat_arg, ..., global_id_list = global_id_list, ...)`.
3. Verify loop_body handles `sel_mat = NULL` correctly when global_id_list is non-NULL (the branch that reads global_id_list shouldn't touch sel_mat).
4. If audit reveals other sel_mat reads inside loop_body: file follow-up before applying.
5. Run `Rscript -e 'devtools::test()' 2>&1 | tail -5`. All 299 tests pass byte-identical.
6. Single commit: `perf(rml): drop redundant sel_mat from .args when global_id_list populated (B9)`.

## V. Output Schema

test_count_pre: 299
test_count_post: 299
daemon_savings_xl: ~60 MB × n_workers
commit_count: 1

## VI. DoD

- [ ] Conditional sel_mat_arg in both dispatch sites
- [ ] All baseline tests pass byte-identical
- [ ] spd.12+13+14 equivalence preserved
- [ ] mw3.3 intact
- [ ] Single commit
close_reason: Merged. 338 tests pass. sel_mat_arg conditional in dispatch: NULL when global_id_list populated. Both args_csrml + args_kset use sel_mat_arg. resolve_global_id_only falls through to global_id_list when sel_mat=NULL.

title: B8: dim()<- instead of matrix() for in-place terml mfh reshape
description:
# B8: Replace matrix() reshape with dim()<- in terml mfh path

## I. Objective
In R/terml.R 3 mfh sites (training hat reshape, predict base reshape, terml_fit hat reshape — all introduced by C.1), replace `hat <- matrix(hat, nrow = 1L)` with `dim(hat) <- c(1L, length(hat))`. R's ALTREP semantics make `dim()<-` more likely to be in-place than `matrix()`, eliminating copy when refcount permits.

## II. Input
- R/terml.R: 3 sites where matrix(x, nrow=1L) reshapes vector→matrix for spd.13 path

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
|------|-------|
| Logic | Result shape identical: 1 × length(x) matrix. Byte-equivalent output to current. |
| Format | 3 surgical line replacements. |
| Boundary | Only R/terml.R. ML backends, wrappers, rml() UNTOUCHED. |
| Audit | C.1 round-trip tests, spd.13 equivalence, mw3.3 invariant all pass byte-identical. |

## IV. Step-by-step

1. `grep -n 'matrix(hat, nrow = 1L)\|matrix(base, nrow = 1L)' R/terml.R` → identify 3 sites.
2. At each site, replace:
   ```r
   hat <- matrix(hat, nrow = 1L)
   ```
   with:
   ```r
   dim(hat) <- c(1L, length(hat))
   ```
   Same for base reshape.
3. Verify in-place behavior: R's `dim()<-` typically modifies refcount-1 vectors in-place. function-arg refcount is usually 2 (caller's reference + local), so a single copy may still occur; the formulation is at least as efficient as matrix(), often better.
4. Run `Rscript -e 'devtools::test()' 2>&1 | tail -5`. All 299 tests pass.
5. Single commit: `perf(terml): use dim()<- instead of matrix() for in-place mfh reshape (B8)`.

## V. Output Schema

test_count_pre: 299
test_count_post: 299
commit_count: 1

- [ ] `grep "dim(hat) <-" R/terml.R` → covers all 3 sites

title: B7: RAM-adaptive qs2 nthreads for checkpoint compression buffer
description:
# B7: RAM-adaptive nthreads for qs2 checkpoint compression

## I. Objective
Replace `nthreads = min(detectCores(), 4L)` in serialize_fit with RAM-adaptive `min(detectCores(), 4L, available_ram_bytes() %/% (256 * 2^20))`. Each qs2 thread allocates ~64 MB zstd buffer; cap nthreads so total buffer (~256 MB at 4 threads) never exceeds RAM headroom.

## II. Input
- R/utils.R: serialize_fit qs2::qs_save call sites
- available_ram_bytes() with F.3 cache + force_refresh

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
|------|-------|
| Logic | Output qs2 file byte-identical regardless of nthreads (zstd deterministic). Test-spd14-internals + spd.13 + spd.12 + checkpoint round-trip all pass byte-identical. |
| Format | Single-helper `qs_nthreads_adaptive()` in utils.R; called from serialize_fit. |
| Boundary | R/utils.R only. ML backends, wrappers, rml() UNTOUCHED. |
| Audit | New test: mock available_ram_bytes to small value (e.g., 100 MB) and verify nthreads resolves to 1. Mock to large (e.g., 8 GB) and verify nthreads resolves to detectCores cap. |

## IV. Step-by-step

1. Add `qs_nthreads_adaptive(max_threads = 4L, buffer_mb_per_thread = 64L, headroom_fraction = 0.5)` to R/utils.R:
   ```r
   qs_nthreads_adaptive <- function(max_threads = 4L, buffer_mb_per_thread = 64L,
                                    headroom_fraction = 0.5) {
     cores <- parallel::detectCores(logical = TRUE)
     if (!is.finite(cores)) cores <- 1L
     ram_bytes <- available_ram_bytes()
     ram_threads_allowed <- (ram_bytes * headroom_fraction) %/% (buffer_mb_per_thread * 2^20)
     max(1L, as.integer(min(cores, max_threads, ram_threads_allowed)))
   }
   ```
2. In serialize_fit: replace `nthreads = min(parallel::detectCores(), 4L)` with `nthreads = qs_nthreads_adaptive()`. Apply at all qs_save call sites (default arm + xgboost arm).
3. Tests in tests/testthat/test-qs-nthreads.R (or extend test-available-ram-cache):
   - Mock `.rml_cache$ram_bytes = 100 * 2^20` (100 MB) → nthreads = 1
   - Mock `8 * 2^30` (8 GB) → nthreads = min(detectCores, 4)
   - Verify qs file byte-identical regardless of nthreads (cross-load test)
4. Run `Rscript -e 'devtools::test()' 2>&1 | tail -5`. All 299+ pass.
5. Single commit: `perf(checkpoint): RAM-adaptive nthreads for qs2 compression buffer (B7)`.

## V. Output Schema
sites_modified: R/utils.R
new_helper: qs_nthreads_adaptive
test_count_pre: 299
test_count_post: >= 302
peak_buffer_bound: <= headroom_fraction × available_ram
commit_count: 1

## VI. DoD
- [ ] qs_nthreads_adaptive returns 1 when RAM tight
- [ ] qs_nthreads_adaptive returns up to 4 when RAM ample
- [ ] qs2 output byte-identical across nthread values (verified)
- [ ] All baseline tests + checkpoint round-trip pass
- [ ] mw3.3 invariant intact
- [ ] Single commit
close_reason: Merged. 367 tests pass. B4: ranger default + ML-aware 0.48 threshold (ranger+RF) + ranger multiplier=5 in estimate_peak_bytes. B7: qs_nthreads_adaptive(). B8: dim()<- for in-place terml mfh. B10: lazy NULL na_cols_list. Epics H/I/J complete.

title: E.5 (spd.24): as.matrix guard at lightgbm predict (xgboost has no such call)
description:
# E.5 (spd.24): as.matrix guard at lightgbm predict (xgboost has no such call)

## I. Context & Objective
In `R/reco_ml.R` rml.lightgbm predict branch (~line 633): guard `as.matrix(Xtest)` with `if (!is.matrix(Xtest)) Xtest <- as.matrix(Xtest)`. Avoids ~300µs + 67KB unnecessary copy per predict call when Xtest is already a matrix. NOTE: rml.xgboost uses `xgb.DMatrix(data = Xtest)` directly — no as.matrix call exists there; scope is lightgbm site only. Reference: plan Epic E.5.

## II. Input Specification
- R/reco_ml.R: rml.lightgbm predict branch only (~line 633)

## III. Constraints & Guards

# B.3 (spd.14): Hoist FoReco2matrix out of per-iter loop_body

## I. Context & Objective
- Objective: Pre-compute FoReco::FoReco2matrix(hat, kset) parts (and analogous transform for mat2hmat) ONCE in rml() outer scope. Pass to loop_body. Per-iter call materializes only requested columns from pre-computed parts.
- Why: spd.12 commit body: "trades per-iter FoReco2matrix cost for closure-size reduction" — this is the perf followup. Both input2rtw_partial and mat2hmat_partial invoke FoReco2matrix per-iter on SAME raw hat. For p=2432 iterations: redundant O(p) transforms.
- Reference: utils.R input2rtw_partial (line 343), mat2hmat_partial (line 322); reco_ml.R loop_body kset branch (137-165), Xtest (195-212); TODO comment at reco_ml.R:140-142.

## II. Input Specification
- R/reco_ml.R rml() outer scope + loop_body
- R/utils.R 2 new internal helpers

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
|------|-------|
| Logic | Numerical output byte-identical. spd.12 + spd.13 equivalence MUST stay green at max_abs_diff == 0. |
| Format | Two new variants: input2rtw_partial_from_parts(parts, kset, cols) and mat2hmat_partial_from_sorted(sorted_vec, h, ncol_total, cols). Originals kept for back-compat. |
| Boundary | reco_ml.R + utils.R only. Wrappers (ctrml/terml/csrml) UNTOUCHED. |
| Audit | spd.12 + spd.13 equivalence stays green; new microbenchmark fixture demonstrates per-iter speedup. |

## IV. Step-by-Step Logic
1. Read current loop_body kset branch.
2. In rml() pre-loop: if !is.null(kset) and is.null(h): parts <- FoReco::FoReco2matrix(hat, kset). If !is.null(h): sorted_vec + ncol_total.
3. Pass to loop_body as additional formal. Same for predict-time base.
4. Add input2rtw_partial_from_parts(parts, kset, cols) and mat2hmat_partial_from_sorted(sorted_vec, h, ncol_total, cols) in utils.R.
5. loop_body kset branch calls *_from_parts / *_from_sorted variants. Originals UNTOUCHED.
6. Update mirai_map .args and sequential .args.
7. Re-run spd.12 + spd.13 equivalence suites. max_abs_diff == 0 for all.
8. Micro-benchmark: dev/spd14-bench.R — p=100 series, kt=21, before vs after. Document in commit body.
9. Single commit: perf(rml): hoist FoReco2matrix and mat2hmat sort out of loop_body (spd.14).

## V. Output Schema

files_unchanged: [R/ctrml.R, R/terml.R, R/csrml.R, R/FoReco.R]

title: B.1: error on pre-existing mirai daemon pool
description:
# B.1: Error on pre-existing mirai daemon pool (spd.1 followup)

## I. Context & Objective
- Objective: Always restore caller mirai daemon state on rml() exit; explicit error when caller has pre-existing pool.
- Why: Current on.exit(mirai::daemons(0)) is conditional on prev == 0L; user with pre-existing pool gets library(FoRecoML) injected with no teardown.
- Reference: reco_ml.R:254-260

## II. Input Specification
- R/reco_ml.R (rml function only)

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
|------|-------|
| Logic | If prev == 0L: register teardown (current). If prev > 0L: cli::cli_abort with actionable message. |
| Format | Surgical change to rml() preamble. |
| Boundary | Only reco_ml.R + 1 new test in test-parallel.R. |
| Tone | Conventional commit. Body explains pool ownership semantics. |

## IV. Step-by-Step Logic
1. Read reco_ml.R:240-280.
2. Replace conditional teardown with explicit error on prev > 0L:
   ```r
   prev <- mirai::status()$connections
   if (prev > 0L) {
     cli::cli_abort(c(
       "Existing mirai daemon pool ({prev} connections) detected.",
       "i" = "rml() requires exclusive daemon ownership.",
       "x" = "Tear down with `mirai::daemons(0)` before calling rml()."
     ))
   }
   mirai::daemons(n_workers_resolved, seed = mirai_seed)
   on.exit(mirai::daemons(0), add = TRUE)
   mirai::everywhere({ library(FoRecoML) })
   ```
3. Add test in test-parallel.R: setup mirai::daemons(1); expect_error(rml(...), "Existing mirai daemon pool"); teardown mirai::daemons(0).
4. devtools::test() -> all 215 + 1 new pass.
5. Single commit: fix(rml): error on pre-existing mirai daemon pool to prevent leakage.

## V. Output Schema
site_modified: R/reco_ml.R lines 254-260
test_added: test-parallel.R::"rml errors on pre-existing daemon pool"
commit_count: 1
test_count_post: >= 216

## VI. Definition of Done
- [ ] prev > 0 case explicit cli_abort with actionable message
- [ ] Test exercises pre-existing pool, asserts error, leaves connections == 0
- [ ] Existing 215 tests still pass
close_reason: Merged. 235 tests pass. rml() now errors with cli_abort when caller has pre-existing mirai daemon pool, preventing library(FoRecoML) injection without teardown.

# Epic B: spd.* maintenance + perf followups

## Goal

title: spd.2: skip na.omit when anyNA(X)==FALSE
description:
# spd.2: Skip na.omit(X) when anyNA(X) is FALSE (v2 — coordinated with spd.1)
**Status:** `READY_FOR_EXECUTION`

## I. Context & Objective
* **Objective:** Guard `X <- na.omit(X)` with `if (anyNA(X)) { ... }` inside `loop_body` (post-spd.1).
* **Why:** na.omit allocates a new matrix unconditionally. Skip when X has no NAs.

## II. Input Specification
R/reco_ml.R only.

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Logic** | When anyNA(X) is FALSE: X unchanged, y unchanged. When TRUE: identical to current behavior. |
| **Boundary** | R/reco_ml.R only. |
| **API** | None. |
| **attr guard form** | Use `!is.null(attr(X, "na.action"))` (matches current code at reco_ml.R:112 which uses `length() > 0` — these are equivalent for na.omit's na.action since na.action is either NULL or non-empty integer vec; either form correct; pick `!is.null` for clarity). |

## IV. Step-by-Step Logic
1. Inside `loop_body` (post-spd.1) where `X <- na.omit(X)` lives, wrap:
   ```r
   if (anyNA(X)) {
     X <- na.omit(X)
     if (!is.null(attr(X, "na.action"))) y <- y[-attr(X, "na.action")]
   }
   ```
2. Run `Rscript -e 'devtools::test()'` → 94+.
3. Numerical equivalence: csrml on clean fixture (no NAs) AND on fixture with hat[1,1] = NA. max_abs_diff = 0.0 both cases.
4. Commit: "spd.2: skip na.omit when anyNA(X)==FALSE"

## V. Output Schema
```toon

success: bool
files_changed: [R/reco_ml.R]
test_result: { passed: int, failed: int }
numerical_check:
  clean_fixture: 0.0

```

## VI. Definition of Done

- [ ] X-mutation and y-mutation both inside guard

- [ ] Numerical identity on clean + NA fixtures

title: Tier-S/A EP: speedup (parallel + per-iter waste)
description:
# Epic: Speedup (hybrid parallelization via mirai + per-iter waste removal)

## Goal
Cut ctrml/csrml/terml wall-clock by parallelizing per-series loop via mirai (spawn-based daemons, avoids fork+thread UB with xgboost/lightgbm/ranger), plus 2 micro-optimizations (na.omit skip; gc throttle).

## Success Criteria
- [ ] spd.1: mirai-based outer parallelization, n_workers="auto", inner-thread cap, deterministic daemon teardown
- [ ] spd.2: na.omit skip when no NAs
- [ ] spd.3: gc every K iters under checkpoint
- [ ] 94 tests pass (85 + 9 new); RNG-aware numerical policy
- [ ] Public API: 1 new optional argument (n_workers)

## Why mirai (not future)
Initial spd.1 attempt used `future::multicore`. Hang observed: fork() after OpenMP/threaded init in xgboost/lightgbm/ranger is undefined behavior — child processes idle indefinitely. `future::multisession` would work but serializes hat/obs/base to each worker (cancels T5/T6 memory wins on POSIX) and has per-task subprocess startup overhead.

mirai (Charlie Gao, NNG-based async/parallel): persistent spawn-based daemons (not fork), single cross-platform API, no L'Ecuyer-CMRG overhead, lighter than future, adopted by purrr ≥1.1 / targets / crew. Sidesteps fork+thread hang outright.

## Execution ordering (MANDATORY)
spd.1 first. spd.2 + spd.3 modify the loop body which spd.1 restructures.

## Sub-Agent Strategy
3 tickets. spd.1 → sonnet (multi-file, integration). spd.2 + spd.3 → haiku (single-file mechanical).
close_reason: EP-sju speedup complete: spd.1 (mirai) + spd.2 (na.omit guard) + spd.3 (gc throttle).

title: T2: Column-wise NA detection (6 sites)
description:
# T2: Column-wise NA detection
**Status:** `READY_FOR_EXECUTION`

## I. Context & Objective
* **Objective:** Replace `na_var <- colSums(is.na(hat)) >= 0.75 * NROW(hat)` with column-wise iteration that does NOT allocate a logical matrix the size of `hat`.
* **Why:** `is.na(hat)` materializes a logical matrix the size of (already row-expanded) `hat`. For ctrml this is 1× of the large training matrix transiently held.
* **Reference Data:** Pattern appears 6 times: R/csrml.R (csrml + csrml_fit), R/terml.R (terml + terml_fit), R/ctrml.R (ctrml + ctrml_fit).
* **Philosophy:** Sub-agent = Goldfish Memory.

Current pattern (identical in all 6 places):
```r
# Remove NA variables from sel_mat
na_var <- colSums(is.na(hat)) >= 0.75 * NROW(hat)
if (any(na_var)) { ... }
```

Target pattern:
```r
na_var <- vapply(
  seq_len(NCOL(hat)),
  function(j) mean(is.na(hat[, j])) >= 0.75,
  logical(1)
)
if (any(na_var)) { ... }
```

For matrix input, `hat[, j]` returns numeric vector (1 col). For Matrix-package sparse Matrix this still works; isolate one column at a time. Peak transient = 1 column instead of full hat.

## II. Input Specification
* **Expected Input:** R source files R/csrml.R, R/terml.R, R/ctrml.R.
* **Format:** R code edits via Edit tool, applied to 6 occurrences.

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Logic** | Resulting `na_var` logical vector must be identical to current behavior (verify on test fixture with NAs). |
| **Format** | Match existing 2-space indent, snake_case. |
| **Boundary** | Touch ONLY R/csrml.R, R/terml.R, R/ctrml.R. |
| **Tone** | Surgical. No drive-by edits. |
| **API** | No public API change. |

## IV. Step-by-Step Logic
1. Grep each file: `grep -n "colSums(is.na(hat))" R/{csrml,terml,ctrml}.R`. Expect 2 hits per file.
2. Replace each with `vapply` form above.
3. Run `devtools::test()`. All pass.
4. Add a small regression check: build a fixture with one all-NA column; verify `na_var[that_col]` is TRUE post-patch. (Add as expectation inside an existing test file at end, or as a manual inline `testthat::test_that` block.)
5. Verify no other use of `is.na(hat)` introduced.

## V. Output Schema (Strict)
```toon
task_id: T2
success: bool
files_changed:
  - path: R/csrml.R
    sites: 2
  - path: R/terml.R
    sites: 2
  - path: R/ctrml.R
    sites: 2
diff: |
  <unified diff>
test_result:
  passed: int
  failed: int
regression_check:
  na_col_detected: bool
error_log: null | msg
```

## VI. Definition of Done
- [ ] 6 occurrences replaced
- [ ] No `colSums(is.na(hat))` remaining in package
- [ ] devtools::test() passes
- [ ] Regression fixture catches all-NA column
close_reason: T2 landed: 0c9f5a3+2d8fe0e -> merged e00335c. 61/61 tests. Followup w30.9 for DRY extraction.

# Epic: EP-B Compact-mode column pruning

## Goal
For `features="compact"` (and other sparse-sel modes), avoid holding full N*m x n*p training matrix when each per-series model uses only a small column subset.

Currently `rml()` holds full hat across the lapply closure even when sel_mat selects only a few columns per series. With compact for ctrml, sel_mat covers ~n columns per HFBTS out of n*p total.

# P2.T5: Docs for method="erm"

## I. Objective
NEWS entry, @param lambda_reg roxygen, vignette section for csrml_g ERM.

## IV. Logic

Learns a LASSO-penalized projection matrix P from in-sample base forecasts. lambda_reg=NULL (default) uses 5-fold cross-validated lambda. title: P3.T1: add nonneg_method param + dispatch to FoReco bpv/nfca/nnic
description:
# P3.T1: nonneg_method via FoReco rec functions (CORRECTED v3)

## I. Context & Objective
- **Objective:** Add `nonneg_method = c("sntz", "bpv", "nfca", "nnic", "osqp")` to all 3 *_g wrappers. When nonneg_method != "sntz", switch from BU path to REC path via `FoReco::csrec/terec/ctrec(nn=nonneg_method, cons_mat=NULL, res=NULL)`.
- **Why:** bpv/nfca/nnic are nn= argument values on csrec/terec/ctrec, not separate exports. sntz remains on the existing BU path for backwards compatibility.

## II. Input Specification
- File: R/rml_g.R
- Verified FoReco contract (live-tested):
  - `csrec(base, agg_mat, cons_mat=NULL, nn=NULL, res=NULL, ...)` — runs with `cons_mat=NULL` and `res=NULL`; auto-derives cons_mat from agg_mat
  - `terec(base, agg_mat, cons_mat=NULL, agg_order, tew="sum", nn=NULL, res=NULL, ...)` — same
  - `ctrec(base, agg_mat, cons_mat=NULL, agg_order, tew="sum", nn=NULL, res=NULL, ...)` — same
  - nn accepts: "osqp", "bpv", "nfca", "nnic", "sntz"
- VERIFICATION command: `Rscript -e 'library(FoReco); agg_mat <- t(c(1,1)); csrec(base=matrix(c(20,10,10), 1, 3), agg_mat=agg_mat, nn="bpv")'` — returns a 1×3 matrix successfully.

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
|------|-------|
| Logic | Default nonneg_method = "sntz" → BU path unchanged (existing csbu/tebu/ctbu with sntz=TRUE) |
| Logic | nonneg_method in {"bpv","nfca","nnic","osqp"} → REC path with FoReco::csrec/terec/ctrec(nn=nonneg_method, cons_mat=NULL, res=NULL) |
| Logic | Pass cons_mat=NULL (FoReco auto-derives from agg_mat). DO NOT compute cons_mat manually. |
| Logic | Pass res=NULL (bpv/nfca/nnic work without residuals; verified live). DO NOT compute training residuals — that's out of scope for P3. |
| Logic | Sparsity heuristic: `mean(bts_vec < 0) > 0.3` AND nonneg_method != "sntz" → cli_warn "Base predictions are >30% negative; {nonneg_method} may produce degenerate face. Consider 'sntz'." |
| Boundary | Don't change BU path semantics. Don't modify rml_g_fit structure to store X_stacked (out of scope; not needed). |
| Forbidden | Do NOT call FoReco::bpv/nfca/nnic directly — they don't exist as exports. |

## IV. Step-by-Step Logic
1. Verify FoReco contract (run verification command above).
2. Add `nonneg_method = c("sntz", "bpv", "nfca", "nnic", "osqp")` to all 3 wrapper signatures.
3. After predict step (bts_vec computed), check sparsity: `sparsity_pct <- mean(bts_vec < 0) * 100`.
4. If nonneg_method != "sntz" AND sparsity_pct > 30 → cli_warn.
5. Branch on nonneg_method:
   - "sntz" → existing BU path (csbu/tebu/ctbu with sntz=TRUE)
   - else → REC path:
     ```r
     # csrml_g
     reco_mat <- FoReco::csrec(base = bts_mat, agg_mat = fit_obj$agg_mat,
                                cons_mat = NULL, nn = nonneg_method, res = NULL)
     # terml_g
     reco_vec <- FoReco::terec(base = matrix(bts_vec, ncol = 1),
                                agg_mat = NULL, cons_mat = NULL,
                                agg_order = fit_obj$agg_order,
                                tew = tew, nn = nonneg_method, res = NULL)
     # ctrml_g
     reco_mat <- FoReco::ctrec(base = t(bts_mat), agg_mat = fit_obj$agg_mat,
                                cons_mat = NULL, agg_order = fit_obj$agg_order,
                                tew = tew, nn = nonneg_method, res = NULL)
     ```
6. Attach attr("FoReco") with new_foreco_info as in existing BU path; framework label unchanged.
7. R CMD INSTALL --preclean . clean.
8. Smoke test:
   ```r
   library(FoRecoML); set.seed(1)
   agg_mat <- t(c(1,1)); dimnames(agg_mat) <- list("A", c("B","C"))
   hat <- matrix(rnorm(150), 50, 3, dimnames = list(NULL, c("A","B","C")))
   obs <- matrix(rnorm(100), 50, 2, dimnames = list(NULL, c("B","C")))
   base <- matrix(rnorm(6), 2, 3, dimnames = list(NULL, c("A","B","C")))
   r_sntz <- csrml_g(base=base, hat=hat, obs=obs, agg_mat=agg_mat, sntz=TRUE, nonneg_method="sntz", seed=1L)
   r_bpv  <- csrml_g(base=base, hat=hat, obs=obs, agg_mat=agg_mat, nonneg_method="bpv", seed=1L)
   stopifnot(is.matrix(r_sntz), is.matrix(r_bpv))
   stopifnot(all(r_sntz >= 0), all(r_bpv >= 0))
   cat("OK sntz vs bpv\n")
   ```
9. Commit: `feat(rml_g): nonneg_method=bpv/nfca/nnic/osqp via FoReco::csrec/terec/ctrec (P3.T1)`

## V. Output Schema
```toon
task_id: P3.T1
success: bool
data:
  files_changed: [R/rml_g.R]
  rec_dispatch_active: bool
  smoke_sntz_nonneg: bool
  smoke_bpv_nonneg: bool
error_log: null
```

## VI. Definition of Done
- [ ] nonneg_method param added to all 3 wrappers
- [ ] REC path branches with cons_mat=NULL, res=NULL
- [ ] Sparsity warning fires above 30%
- [ ] sntz behavior unchanged
- [ ] Both smoke tests pass (sntz + bpv both produce non-negative output)
- [ ] Conventional commit
close_reason: nonneg_method=bpv/nfca/nnic/osqp via FoReco nn= arg; PASS 279; sparsity warning; sntz BU path unchanged

title: P2.T2: fit_erm() helper — Kronecker LASSO for projection matrix P
description:
# P2.T2: fit_erm() — Kronecker LASSO for projection matrix P

## I. Objective
Add `fit_erm(hat, obs, S, lambda_reg=NULL)` to R/utils.R. Fits P (n_bottom × n_all) via LASSO on Kronecker system derived from vec identity.

## II. Mathematical basis (verified live)
ERM model: Y ≈ S P Yhat where Y = n_all × T_obs actuals, Yhat = n_all × T_obs base forecasts.

Via vec(ABC) = (C' ⊗ A)vec(B):
- vec(Y) = vec(S P Yhat) = (Yhat' ⊗ S) vec(P) = (hat ⊗ S) vec(P)
- X_design = kronecker(hat, S) — shape (T_obs×n_all) × (n_all×n_bot) = T_obs*n_all × n_all*n_bot
- y_vec = as.vector(Y_full) — length n_all*T_obs ✓ (matches nrow(X_design))
- vec(P) has length n_bot*n_all = ncol(X_design) ✓

VERIFIED: `dim(kronecker(hat, S))` = c(T_obs*n_all, n_all*n_bot); nrow(X) == length(y_vec) = TRUE

## III. Input Specification
- hat: T_obs × n_all (in-sample base forecasts, ALL series)
- obs: T_obs × n_bot (bottom-level actuals only)
- S: summation matrix n_all × n_bot from cstools(agg_mat)$strc_mat or tetools/cttools
- lambda_reg: numeric or NULL

## IV. Constraints & Guards
| Logic | Y_full = rbind(S[seq_len(n_all-n_bot),] %*% t(obs), t(obs)) — n_all × T_obs |
| Logic | X_design = kronecker(hat, S) — NOT kronecker(S, t(hat)) |
| Logic | lambda_reg=NULL → cv.glmnet(X, y_vec, alpha=1, nfolds=5, intercept=FALSE) |
| Logic | lambda_reg numeric → glmnet(X, y_vec, alpha=1, lambda=lambda_reg, intercept=FALSE) |
| Logic | Coef extraction: as.numeric(coef(fit))[-1] or coef(fit, s="lambda.min")[-1] — drop intercept slot (glmnet returns (ncol+1) coefs even with intercept=FALSE; index 1 = intercept = 0) |
| Logic | P = matrix(P_vec, nrow=n_bot, ncol=n_all) |
| Boundary | Add glmnet to DESCRIPTION Suggests; abort if not installed |
| Forbidden | No MASS; no custom LASSO |

## V. Logic
```r
fit_erm <- function(hat, obs, S, lambda_reg = NULL) {
  if (!requireNamespace("glmnet", quietly = TRUE))
    cli_abort("method='erm' requires {.pkg glmnet}. Install with {.run install.packages('glmnet')}.")
  n_all <- nrow(S)
  n_bot <- ncol(S)
  T_obs <- nrow(hat)
  # Full actuals (aggregate rows = S[1:na,] @ t(obs))
  Y_full <- rbind(S[seq_len(n_all - n_bot), , drop = FALSE] %*% t(obs),
                  t(obs))                                                   # n_all × T_obs
  y_vec  <- as.vector(Y_full)                                               # T_obs*n_all
  X      <- kronecker(hat, S)                                               # T_obs*n_all × n_all*n_bot
  # Fit LASSO
  P_vec <- if (is.null(lambda_reg)) {
    fit <- glmnet::cv.glmnet(X, y_vec, alpha = 1L, nfolds = 5L,
                              intercept = FALSE)
    as.numeric(coef(fit, s = "lambda.min"))[-1L]
  } else {
    fit <- glmnet::glmnet(X, y_vec, alpha = 1L, lambda = lambda_reg,
                          intercept = FALSE)
    as.numeric(coef(fit))[-1L]
  }
  matrix(P_vec, nrow = n_bot, ncol = n_all)
}
```

Also: add `glmnet` to DESCRIPTION Suggests field.

## VI. Schema
```toon
task_id: P2.T2
data:
  helper_added: fit_erm
  glmnet_in_suggests: bool
  smoke_dim: [n_bot, n_all]
```

## VII. DoD
- [ ] fit_erm() in R/utils.R
- [ ] glmnet in DESCRIPTION Suggests
- [ ] Abort if glmnet absent (skip_if_not_installed in tests)
- [ ] lambda_reg=NULL → cv.glmnet; numeric → glmnet
- [ ] Smoke: matrix(nrow=n_bot, ncol=n_all)
- [ ] R CMD INSTALL clean; devtools::test() FAIL 0
- [ ] Conventional commit
close_reason: CANCELLED: parent epic cancelled after empirical validation

title: P2.T3: integrate method='erm' into csrml_g/terml_g/ctrml_g
description:
# P2.T3: integrate method="erm" into all 3 wrappers

## I. Objective
Replace cli_abort stubs with real ERM path for csrml_g, terml_g, and ctrml_g.

## II. Verified S matrices and reshape rules

### csrml_g
- S = cstools(agg_mat)$strc_mat — n_cs × n_cs_bot (e.g. 3 × 2)
- hat: T_obs × n_cs (no reshape needed)
- obs: T_obs × n_cs_bot (no reshape needed)
- Y_full: n_cs × T_obs
- X: kronecker(hat, S) → T_obs*n_cs × n_cs*n_cs_bot

### terml_g
- S = tetools(agg_order)$strc_mat — kt × m (e.g. 7 × 4; kt = sum(m/k), m = max(agg_order))
- hat: T_obs × kt (no reshape needed — each row is one high-freq period with kt temporal features)
- obs: T_obs × 1 (single bottom series at highest freq)
- Y_full: kt × T_obs
- X: kronecker(hat, S) → T_obs*kt × kt*m

### ctrml_g (KEY: reshape needed)
- S = cttools(agg_mat, agg_order)$strc_mat — (n_cs*kt) × (n_cs_bot*m) (e.g. 21 × 8)
- hat: T_obs × (n_cs*kt) — existing hat structure. Reshape to T_cycles × (n_cs*kt) where T_cycles = T_obs/m.
  ```r
  m <- max(agg_order)
  T_cycles <- nrow(hat) / m   # T_obs/m high-freq cycles
  hat_reshaped <- hat[seq(m, nrow(hat), by=m), , drop=FALSE]  # take every m-th row (cycle endpoints)
  ```
  NOTE: This is a simplification — see IV Logic for the correct aggregation.
- obs: T_obs × n_cs_bot (bottom CS series at highest freq). Expand to T_cycles × (n_cs_bot*m):
  ```r
  obs_mat <- matrix(as.vector(obs), nrow = T_cycles, ncol = n_cs_bot * m, byrow = FALSE)
  ```
- Y_full: (n_cs*kt) × T_cycles
- X: kronecker(hat_reshaped, S) → T_cycles*(n_cs*kt) × (n_cs*kt)*(n_cs_bot*m) ✓

## III. Guards
| Logic | lambda_reg param added to all 3 wrappers |
| Logic | cstools/tetools/cttools: use $strc_mat NOT $S |
| Logic | ctrml_g: T_obs must be divisible by m = max(agg_order); cli_abort if not |
| Logic | Memory warning: nrow(hat_for_erm)*nrow(S)*ncol(S) > 5e6 → cli_warn |
| Boundary | Don't touch BU/rec paths |

## IV. Logic (ctrml_g reshape)
```r
if (method == "erm") {
  m <- max(agg_order)
  T_obs <- nrow(hat)
  if (T_obs %% m != 0L)
    cli_abort("{.arg hat} must have rows divisible by max(agg_order)={m} for method='erm'.", call=NULL)
  T_cycles <- T_obs / m
  n_cs_bot <- ncol(obs)
  # Reshape hat to T_cycles rows: average over each m-period cycle per column
  hat_cyc <- matrix(0, T_cycles, ncol(hat))
  for (i in seq_len(T_cycles)) {
    idx <- ((i-1)*m + 1):(i*m)
    hat_cyc[i, ] <- colMeans(hat[idx, , drop=FALSE])
  }
  # Expand obs to T_cycles × (n_cs_bot*m): each row is one cycle's bottom obs flattened
  obs_cyc <- matrix(as.vector(t(
    matrix(as.vector(obs), nrow=m, ncol=T_cycles*n_cs_bot)
  )), nrow=T_cycles, ncol=n_cs_bot*m, byrow=FALSE)
  S <- FoReco::cttools(agg_mat=agg_mat, agg_order=agg_order)$strc_mat
  P <- fit_erm(hat=hat_cyc, obs=obs_cyc, S=S, lambda_reg=lambda_reg)
  reco_mat <- S %*% P %*% t(base)  # (n_cs*kt) × h; base is h × (n_cs*kt)
  ...
}
```

## V. Schema
```toon
task_id: P2.T3
data:
  csrml_g_erm: bool
  terml_g_erm: bool
  ctrml_g_erm: bool
  smoke_all_three: bool
```

## VI. DoD
- [ ] csrml_g method="erm" → n_cs × h matrix
- [ ] terml_g method="erm" → kt × h matrix (or compatible named vector)
- [ ] ctrml_g method="erm" → (n_cs*kt) × h matrix; T_obs divisible by m guard
- [ ] lambda_reg on all 3 wrappers
- [ ] strc_mat used in all 3
- [ ] FAIL 0; conventional commit

comments.author: Dennis Alexis Valin Dittrich
comments.text:
RESEARCH COMPLETE (2026-05-16). ERM summary:

Paper: Ben Taieb & Koo (2019) "Regularized regression for hierarchical forecasting without unbiasedness conditions" KDD '19. doi:10.1145/3292500.3330976

Algorithm:
1. Construct full actuals: Y_insample (n_all × T_obs) = rbind(agg_mat %*% t(obs), t(obs))
2. Design matrix: X = kronecker(S, t(hat)) — shape: (n_all × T_obs, n_bottom × n_all) flattened = T_obs*n_all × n_bottom*n_all
3. Response: y_vec = as.vector(Y_insample) — length T_obs*n_all
4. Fit P_vec via glmnet(X, y_vec, alpha=1, lambda=lambda_reg) — LASSO
5. Reshape P_vec to P (n_bottom × n_all)
6. Prediction: reconciled = S %*% P %*% t(base) — shape n_all × h

Variants: closed (no regularization, pinv), reg (LASSO), reg_bu (LASSO penalizing deviation from BU)

Key params: lambda_reg (default auto = max(abs(t(X) %*% y_vec)) / T_obs)

R deps: glmnet (LASSO), MASS (ginv for closed variant)

NOT covered by FoReco comb=sam/shr (those are MinT-GLS; ERM drops unbiasedness assumption).

method="erm" design: bypasses ML step entirely. User provides hat, obs, agg_mat, base, lambda_reg.

title: epic(P3): expert non-negative methods bpv/nfca/nnic on *_g (opt-in)
description:
# Epic P3: Non-Negative Method Threading (Expert Opt-In)

## Goal
Thread FoReco's bpv/nfca/nnic non-negative reconciliation algorithms through *_g wrappers behind `nonneg_method = c("sntz", "bpv", "nfca", "nnic")` flag, with explicit warning about degenerate-face risk on sparse hierarchies.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `*_g(sntz=TRUE, nonneg_method="bpv"/"nfca"/"nnic")` dispatches to FoReco implementation
- [ ] sntz remains default (near-optimal per Girolimetto 2025)
- [ ] Documentation warns about sparse-hierarchy degenerate face risk

## Context & Background
Design doc: docs/research/2026-05-16-ctrml-g-recommendations-prioritized.md §5 demotion. Girolimetto (2025 Forecasting 7(4):64) proves sntz near-optimal.

The 3 alternatives (bpv = block-pivoting, nfca = non-negative least-squares with constraints, nnic = non-negative iterative correction) are expert options for specific distributional settings. On hierarchies with 30-40% structural zeros, projection surface becomes ill-conditioned. sntz (clip negatives after BU) avoids this entirely.

## Prerequisites
- After P0 + P1 ship
- Low priority — sntz is sufficient for most users

## Sub-Agent Strategy
- T1: add `nonneg_method` param; wire to FoReco bpv/nfca/nnic dispatch
- T2: emit warning when sparse + non-sntz method selected
- T3: tests on dense + sparse fixtures
- T4: docs

## Status
BACKLOG — lowest priority
close_reason: P3 epic shipped: nonneg_method dispatch + docs. T2 tests deferred.

comments.author: Dennis Alexis Valin Dittrich
comments.text: CORRECTION (gate iter 1): bpv/nfca/nnic are NOT separate FoReco exports. They are nn= argument values on FoReco::csrec/terec/ctrec (linear rec path). P3.T1 updated to use this dispatch. Implication: non-sntz nonneg_method switches *_g from BU path (csbu/tebu/ctbu) to REC path (csrec/terec/ctrec). Verified via library(FoReco); ls() showing only set_bounds + ?csrec docs.

title: epic(P2): ERM linear projection method='erm' (HierarchicalForecast-style)
description:
# Epic P2: ERM Reconciliation for all *_g wrappers

## Goal
Add `method = "erm"` to csrml_g, terml_g, ctrml_g — LASSO-penalized projection matrix P learned from in-sample (hat, obs) data.

## Algorithm (Ben Taieb & Koo 2019, KDD)
For each framework, S from FoReco tools ($strc_mat field), then:
- X = kronecker(hat_reshaped, S)
- P via glmnet LASSO
- reconciled = S @ P @ t(base)

## Framework-specific reshape
- csrml_g: no reshape; hat T_obs × n_cs, obs T_obs × n_cs_bot
- terml_g: no reshape; hat T_obs × kt, obs T_obs × 1
- ctrml_g: reshape hat → T_cycles × (n_cs*kt), obs → T_cycles × (n_cs_bot*m)

## Verified (live)
- kronecker(hat, S) dimensions correct for cs and ct cases
- $strc_mat field exists in cstools/tetools/cttools

## Sub-Agent Strategy
T2 → T3 → T4 → T5

title: F.3: cache available_ram_bytes per session
description:
# F.3: cache available_ram_bytes per session

## I. Context & Objective
In `R/utils.R` `available_ram_bytes`: cache result in package-internal env on first call. RAM doesn't change materially during session. Saves ~50µs syscall per rml() call (reads /proc/meminfo each time currently). Reference: plan Epic F.3.

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
|------|-------|
| Logic | Cache in package-internal env (e.g. `.pkg_cache`). First call reads /proc/meminfo, caches; subsequent calls return cached. Provide force_refresh=FALSE param for testing. |
| Format | Internal env + memoized function. |
| Boundary | R/utils.R available_ram_bytes function only. |
| Audit | Test: call twice, second call returns same value without syscall (mock /proc/meminfo). force_refresh=TRUE invalidates cache. |

## IV. Step-by-step Logic
1. Define `.ram_cache <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())` at package load (zzz.R or utils.R top level).
2. In available_ram_bytes(): check `.ram_cache$bytes`; return if set.
3. On cache miss: read /proc/meminfo, store in `.ram_cache$bytes`, return.
4. Add `force_refresh` param for testing.
5. Single commit: `perf(utils): cache available_ram_bytes per session (F.3)`.

## VI. DoD
- [ ] available_ram_bytes() reads /proc/meminfo only on first call per session
- [ ] Second call returns cached value (test via call count mock or timing)
- [ ] force_refresh=TRUE param invalidates cache
- [ ] All baseline tests pass
- [ ] mw3.3 invariant intact
- [ ] Single commit; conventional commits; no AI attribution
close_reason: Merged. 299 tests pass. F.1: gc_every n_workers-aware. F.2: dimnames simplified. F.3: RAM cached w/ force_refresh.

# F.1: n_workers-aware gc_every

In `R/reco_ml.R` loop_body: derive `gc_every` from n_workers_resolved instead of hardcoded `5L`. More workers → less memory headroom → more frequent GC. Formula: `gc_every <- max(2L, 10L %/% n_workers_resolved)`. Reference: plan Epic F.1.

|------|-------|

| Format | Derive gc_every in outer scope; pass via .args. |
| Boundary | R/reco_ml.R gc_every computation only. |
| Audit | Test formula values for n_workers=1,2,4,10. |

title: spd.3: gc() every K iters under checkpoint
description:
# spd.3: Reduce gc() frequency under checkpoint mode (v2 — coordinated with spd.1 loop_body)
**Status:** `READY_FOR_EXECUTION`

## I. Context & Objective
* **Objective:** Per-iter `gc()` under checkpoint mode runs every iteration. Replace with periodic gc (every 5 iters). Define `gc_every` INSIDE `loop_body` (so it's accessible after spd.1 restructures the for-loop into a parallelizable function).
* **Why:** gc() takes 50-200ms. For p=100 cheap fits, gc overhead = 5-20s. Cut by 5×.
* **Forbidden:** Defining `gc_every` in `rml()` outer scope only — that variable won't be visible inside `loop_body` after spd.1 refactor (spawn daemons don't inherit). Must be either inside loop_body or in .args.

## II. Input Specification
R/reco_ml.R only.

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Logic** | Memory peak bounded within 5× single-model footprint under checkpoint. |
| **Boundary** | R/reco_ml.R only. |
| **API** | None. |
| **Scope-safe under spd.1** | `gc_every` defined INSIDE `loop_body` (function-local), NOT outer `rml()` scope. Each daemon's loop_body has its own constant. |

## IV. Step-by-Step Logic
1. Inside `loop_body` (post-spd.1), at the top of the function:
   ```r
   loop_body <- function(i, hat, obs, base, sel_mat, col_map, class_base,
                         approach, active_ncol, params, fit, checkpoint_dir, dots) {
     gc_every <- 5L
     # ... existing body ...
   }
   ```
2. Replace the per-iter gc() call:
   ```r
   if (!is.null(checkpoint_dir)) {
     gc(verbose = FALSE)
   }
   ```
   with:
   ```r
   if (!is.null(checkpoint_dir) && i %% gc_every == 0L) {
     gc(verbose = FALSE)
   }
   ```
3. Note: with spd.1 in place, each daemon evaluates `loop_body` independently. The `i %% gc_every` counter is on the global iteration index `i` (1..p) passed via mirai_map's `.x`. Daemons hit i values 1, 2, 3, ... in arbitrary order; each daemon will trigger gc when its local i is divisible by 5. Equivalent total gc count across full run.
4. No post-loop gc in rml() (each daemon manages its own GC; orchestrator's gc is automatic).
5. Run `Rscript -e 'devtools::test()'` → 94+ pass (after spd.1 + spd.3).
6. Numerical equivalence: csrml × randomForest × checkpoint=tempfile() on p>=10 fixture. max_abs_diff = 0.0 vs baseline.
7. Commit: "spd.3: gc() every 5 iters under checkpoint mode (inside loop_body for spd.1 compat)"

## V. Output Schema
```toon
task_id: spd.3
success: bool
files_changed: [R/reco_ml.R]
test_result: { passed: int, failed: int }
gc_every_constant: 5
gc_every_scope: "inside loop_body"   # NOT outer rml()
numerical_check: { checkpoint_path: 0.0 }
```

## VI. Definition of Done
- [ ] `gc_every <- 5L` defined INSIDE loop_body (function-local)
- [ ] Per-iter gc gated on (`i %% gc_every == 0L`)
- [ ] No `gc_every` reference in outer rml() scope
- [ ] 94+ tests pass
- [ ] Numerical output byte-identical with checkpoint enabled
close_reason: Landed: dc37e8a -> merged. gc_every=5L inside loop_body; gc gated on i %% 5 == 0.

title: Dedup fit references in rml() return path
description:
# mw3.2: Dedup fit references in rml() return path
**Status:** `READY_FOR_EXECUTION`

## I. Context & Objective
* **Objective:** Replace `do.call("rbind", out) + ml_step[, "fit"] + ml_step[, "bts"]` chain with direct lapply extraction. Drop intermediate matrix-of-lists `ml_step`. PRESERVE existing `is.null(fit)` guard around `fit$fit <- ...`.
* **Why:** Three concurrent references hold the fit list at end of training: `out`, `ml_step`, `fit$fit`. R refcounts (no deep copy) but `ml_step` keeps mlr3/xgboost backend pointers alive longer than necessary. Removing `ml_step` releases sooner.
* **Mechanism:** Direct `lapply(out, [[, "fit")` and `lapply(out, [[, "bts")`. Both produce unnamed lists of length p — shape-equivalent to current `ml_step[, "fit"]` and `ml_step[, "bts"]` (each extracts an unnamed list-column; `do.call("list", x)` on an unnamed list is identity in shape, just rewraps).
* **Forbidden:** Moving `fit$fit <- ...` outside the existing `if (is.null(fit))` guard.
* **Reference Data (verified HEAD 5984fca):**
  - R/reco_ml.R:161 `ml_step <- do.call("rbind", out)`
  - R/reco_ml.R:162-172 `if (is.null(fit)) { fit <- NULL; fit$sel_mat <- sel_mat; fit$fit <- do.call("list", ml_step[, "fit"]); fit$approach <- class_base; fit$checkpoint_dir <- checkpoint_dir; class(fit) <- "rml_fit" }`
  - R/reco_ml.R:174-179 `if (!is.null(base)) { bts <- do.call("cbind", ml_step[, "bts"]); attr(bts, "fit") <- fit; return(bts) } else { return(fit) }`

## II. Input Specification
R/reco_ml.R only.

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Logic** | `bts` content + dim identical. `fit$fit` set ONLY when `is.null(fit)` (training). On predict-reuse, caller-supplied `fit$fit` (checkpoint paths) MUST remain unchanged. |
| **Shape** | `lapply(out, [[, "fit")` produces unnamed length-p list; verify equivalent to `do.call("list", ml_step[, "fit"])` (both unnamed length-p lists of fit objects/paths). Note: `ml_step[, "fit"]` is a list-column of class "list"; `do.call("list", x)` on it is `as.list(x)` — identity for unnamed list inputs. Shape preserved. |
| **Boundary** | R/reco_ml.R only. |
| **API** | None. |

## IV. Step-by-Step Logic
1. Replace lines 161-179 with this exact block (note braces):
   ```r
   if (is.null(fit)) {
     fit <- NULL
     fit$sel_mat <- sel_mat
     fit$fit <- lapply(out, `[[`, "fit")
     fit$approach <- class_base
     fit$checkpoint_dir <- checkpoint_dir
     class(fit) <- "rml_fit"
   }

   if (!is.null(base)) {
     bts <- do.call(cbind, lapply(out, `[[`, "bts"))
     rm(out)
     attr(bts, "fit") <- fit
     return(bts)
   } else {
     rm(out)
     return(fit)
   }
   ```
2. Confirm `ml_step` is fully removed (no later reference).
3. Run `devtools::test()` → 84/84 pass.
4. Numerical equivalence check (set.seed=42; ctrml + csrml + terml × randomForest/xgboost/lightgbm × features [all, str-bts, compact where applicable]); max_abs_diff = 0.0.

## V. Output Schema
```toon
task_id: mw3.2
success: bool
files_changed: [R/reco_ml.R]
test_result: { passed: int, failed: int }
ml_step_removed: bool
fit_fit_guard_preserved: bool
shape_equivalence_verified: bool
numerical_check: { max_abs_diff: 0.0 }
```

## VI. Definition of Done
- [ ] `ml_step` removed from rml()
- [ ] `fit$fit <- lapply(...)` stays inside `if (is.null(fit))` guard
- [ ] `do.call(cbind, lapply(out, [[, "bts"))` replaces `do.call("cbind", ml_step[, "bts"])`
- [ ] `rm(out)` after extraction
- [ ] 84/84 tests pass
- [ ] Numerical output byte-identical

## Appendix: Test scope
This ticket does NOT add a new regression test. Earlier draft (v2) proposed a check on `attr(bts, "fit")$fit` type, but the existing `tests/testthat/test-checkpoint.R:80` already verifies that `mdl$fit` is a character vector on `checkpoint=path` runs — same property, same path. Adding a duplicate test would not improve coverage. The `is.null(fit)` guard preservation (this ticket's primary invariant) is verified via existing test-checkpoint.R suite + the new mw3.3 round-trip test.
close_reason: Landed: 6697b4e -> merged. 85/85 tests; ml_step removed; guard preserved.

title: Drop fit_i after bts in predict-reuse loop
description:
# mw3.3: Drop fit_i after bts extraction in predict-reuse loop
**Status:** `READY_FOR_EXECUTION`

## I. Context & Objective
* **Objective:** On `rml()` predict-reuse (`!is.null(fit)`), lambda returns only `list(bts = bts)` — drop `fit` field so deserialized models do not accumulate across the lapply.
* **Why:** T6 final-review: `get_fit_i(fit, i)` lazy-loads each model at R/reco_ml.R:126. Lambda returns `list(bts=bts, fit=fit_i)`. `out[[i]] <- tmp` keeps the model alive via `out[[i]]$fit`. Line 155 `rm(X, y, Xtest, fit_i)` IS unconditional and DOES release the loop-local `fit_i` binding — but `out[[i]]$fit` retains the model reference until `out` itself is released after the loop. All p deserialized models live concurrently until lapply completes.
* **Mechanism:** Conditional return shape at lambda end: `if (is.null(fit)) list(bts=bts, fit=fit_obj) else list(bts=bts)`.
* **Forbidden:** Adding `rm(fit_i)` to the new branch — line 155 already covers it. No double-free.
* **Reference Data (verified HEAD 5984fca):**
  - R/reco_ml.R:92 `out <- vector("list", p)`
  - R/reco_ml.R:103-126 lapply body with branches
  - R/reco_ml.R:154 `out[[i]] <- tmp`
  - R/reco_ml.R:155 `rm(X, y, Xtest, fit_i)` unconditional both paths

## II. Input Specification
R/reco_ml.R + tests/testthat/test-checkpoint.R.

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Logic** | reco_mat unchanged. `rml_fit$fit` (caller checkpoint paths) untouched. |
| **Boundary** | R/reco_ml.R + tests/testthat/test-checkpoint.R only. |
| **API** | None. |
| **No double-free** | Line 155 unchanged. |
| **Cross-ticket** | Compatible with mw3.2: on reuse, `out[[i]]` has only `bts` field. mw3.2's `lapply(out, [[, "bts")` (gated by `!is.null(base)`) is safe — `bts` present on both paths. mw3.2's `lapply(out, [[, "fit")` is gated by `is.null(fit)` — only runs training path where `out[[i]]$fit` exists. |

## IV. Step-by-Step Logic
1. Locate lambda body in `rml()`. At lambda end, find `return(list(bts=..., fit=...))` and replace with:
   ```r
   if (is.null(fit)) {
     list(bts = bts, fit = fit_obj)   # use actual local name from existing code
   } else {
     list(bts = bts)
   }
   ```
2. Line 155 `rm(X, y, Xtest, fit_i)` UNCHANGED.
3. Add mandatory correctness regression test in `tests/testthat/test-checkpoint.R`:
   ```r
   test_that("predict-reuse numerical equivalence with checkpointed fit (mw3.3)", {
     skip_if_not_installed("qs2")
     skip_if_not_installed("randomForest")
     set.seed(42)
     fx <- <fixture from existing helpers>
     mdl_mem <- csrml_fit(hat = fx$hat, obs = fx$obs, agg_mat = fx$agg_mat,
                          approach = "randomForest")
     set.seed(42)
     td <- tempfile(); dir.create(td)
     mdl_disk <- csrml_fit(hat = fx$hat, obs = fx$obs, agg_mat = fx$agg_mat,
                           approach = "randomForest", checkpoint = td)
     r_mem  <- csrml(base = fx$base, fit = mdl_mem)
     r_disk <- csrml(base = fx$base, fit = mdl_disk)
     expect_equal(as.numeric(r_disk), as.numeric(r_mem), tolerance = 1e-12)
     unlink(td, recursive = TRUE)
   })
   ```
   This test (a) exercises the predict-reuse code path edited by this ticket, (b) verifies numerical equivalence vs in-memory baseline, (c) does NOT attempt to measure internal memory accumulation (which is not observable from R-level test code without rewriting rml()).
4. Memory behavior (the actual accumulation reduction) is verified via code review of the conditional return shape, not a unit test. Document this in the commit message:
   ```
   Memory reduction verified by inspection: on reuse path, lambda returns
   list(bts = bts) only; out[[i]] no longer retains deserialized model.
   Behavioral test asserts predict-reuse numerical correctness; internal
   memory pressure is not observable from R-level test code.
   ```
5. `devtools::test()` → 85/85 pass (84 existing + 1 new).
6. Commit: "mw3.3: drop tmp$fit on predict-reuse to release deserialized models in-loop"

## V. Output Schema

title: Drop cosmetic no-op wrappers
description:
# tvy.1: Drop cosmetic no-op wrappers (as.vector / rownames / unname)
**Status:** `READY_FOR_EXECUTION`

## I. Context & Objective
* **Objective:** Remove 3 categories of no-op wrappers in R/reco_ml.R.
* **Why:** Each micro-allocation. Cumulative + cleanup.
* **Forbidden:** Touching xgb/lgb predict argument coercion (already correct post-T1); changing predict semantics.

## Reference Data (verified HEAD 6c32efe)

### Category A: `as.vector(predict(fit, ...))` → `predict(fit, ...)` (3 live sites)
predict() for randomForest/xgboost/lightgbm regression returns numeric vector. `as.vector()` on numeric vector is identity-copy.
- R/reco_ml.R:328 `bts <- as.vector(predict(fit, Xtest))` (rml.randomForest)
- R/reco_ml.R:393 `bts <- as.vector(predict(fit, test))` (rml.xgboost; `test` is DMatrix)
- R/reco_ml.R:460 `bts <- as.vector(predict(fit, Xtest))` (rml.lightgbm)
Commented-out sites at 502/549/595 left alone.

### Category B: `rownames(X) <- NULL` (1 live site)
- R/reco_ml.R:238 inside rml.mlr3 block_sampling branch. Post-T1, X is numeric matrix → rownames already NULL → no-op assignment.
- Decision: drop the line. If vu2.1 lands first and keeps it for parity, this ticket removes it. If vu2.1 hasn't landed, this ticket removes it before vu2.1 sees it.

### Category C: `unname(base)` (1 site)
- R/reco_ml.R:57 `base <- unname(base)` — same pattern mw3.1 fixed for hat/obs. Replace with in-place dimnames/names removal.

## II. Input Specification
R/reco_ml.R ONLY.

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Logic** | predict outputs as numeric vector at all 3 sites (verify by inspecting model class). bts content + dim unchanged. |
| **Boundary** | R/reco_ml.R only. |
| **API** | None. |
| **Verify per-category** | Before each removal, confirm no-op via local inspection. |

## IV. Step-by-Step Logic
1. **Category A — as.vector(predict)**:
   - Verify each predict call returns vector. For randomForest with regression: yes. For xgboost: yes (1D regression output). For lightgbm: yes (regression returns vector).
   - Drop `as.vector(...)` wrapper at lines 328, 393, 460:
     - `bts <- as.vector(predict(fit, Xtest))` → `bts <- predict(fit, Xtest)`
     - same shape for line 393 and 460.
2. **Category B — rownames(X) <- NULL**:
   - Verify X type at line 238: X is the matrix passed into the lambda from `rml()`, never a data.frame post-T1. `rownames(NULL-rowname-matrix)` returns NULL → assignment is no-op.
   - Drop line 238 entirely.
3. **Category C — unname(base)**:
   - Replace line 57 `base <- unname(base)` with:
     ```r
     if (!is.null(names(base))) names(base) <- NULL
     if (!is.null(dimnames(base))) dimnames(base) <- NULL
     ```
   - Same pattern as mw3.1 fix for hat/obs.
4. Run `Rscript -e 'devtools::test()'` → 85/85.
5. Numerical equivalence: set.seed(42); csrml/terml/ctrml × randomForest/xgboost/lightgbm; max_abs_diff = 0.0.

title: Drop obs t()+unname() copies
description:
# mw3.1: Drop redundant obs/hat copies in ctrml/terml + rml()
**Status:** `READY_FOR_EXECUTION`

## I. Context & Objective
* **Objective:** Eliminate redundant obs/hat copies along wrapper → rml(). Currently rml() entry does `hat <- unname(hat)` + `obs <- unname(obs)` regardless of whether wrappers already strip dimnames.
* **Why:** ctrml non-mfh path: `obs <- t(obs)` (R/ctrml.R:282, :588) — transpose copies; then `obs <- unname(obs)` in rml() — second copy. obs size = nb × N*m. Two transient copies per call.
* **Mechanism:** Replace `unname()` (always allocates duplicate) with `dimnames(x) <- NULL` (in-place when refcount=1).
* **Reference Data (verified HEAD 5984fca):**
  - R/reco_ml.R:33 `hat <- unname(hat)`
  - R/reco_ml.R:34 `obs <- unname(obs)`
  - R/ctrml.R:282 / R/ctrml.R:588 `obs <- t(obs)` (non-mfh training + _fit)
  - R/terml.R:195 / R/terml.R:444 `obs <- cbind(obs)` (non-mfh)
  - R/csrml.R: NO obs transpose. obs passed directly to rml() at R/csrml.R:265 (`obs = obs`). Confirmed via grep — only mentions are a doc example (line 75) and predict-reuse path setting `obs <- NULL` (line 245). mw3.1 unname-removal still safe for csrml because user-supplied obs may carry names; `dimnames<-NULL` handles it correctly.
  - mfh branches (e.g., R/ctrml.R:284, R/terml.R:197) reshape obs via `matrix(...)` rather than `t()` — out of scope for this ticket; revisit if needed.

## II. Input Specification
R/reco_ml.R only (preferred minimal). Optionally R/ctrml.R + R/terml.R if folding wrapper t/cbind with the in-place dimnames is worthwhile (only if it reduces copies further).

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Logic** | Per-series y = obs[, i] (or equivalent) byte-identical pre/post across ALL approaches × ALL features. |
| **Boundary** | R/reco_ml.R primary; optional touchpoints in R/ctrml.R + R/terml.R only if needed. |
| **API** | None. |
| **csrml safety** | csrml passes obs raw → dimnames-stripping in rml() is necessary (user obs may carry names). |

## IV. Step-by-Step Logic
1. Replace R/reco_ml.R:33-34:
   ```r
   hat <- unname(hat)
   obs <- unname(obs)
   ```
   with:
   ```r
   if (!is.null(dimnames(hat))) dimnames(hat) <- NULL
   if (!is.null(dimnames(obs))) dimnames(obs) <- NULL
   ```
   The `is.null(dimnames(x))` check avoids the trivial assignment when already stripped (cheap).
2. Numerical equivalence: set.seed(42); fixture from man/csrml.Rd + man/ctrml.Rd + man/terml.Rd; all 4 approaches × applicable features for each wrapper; max_abs_diff = 0.0.
3. `devtools::test()` → 84/84.

## V. Output Schema
```toon

success: bool
files_changed: [R/reco_ml.R]
test_result: { passed: int, failed: int }
numerical_check:
  csrml: { approaches: [...], features: [...], max_abs_diff: 0.0 }
  ctrml: { approaches: [...], features: [...], max_abs_diff: 0.0 }
  terml: { approaches: [...], features: [...], max_abs_diff: 0.0 }
```

## VI. Definition of Done

- [ ] 84/84 tests pass
- [ ] Numerical output byte-identical across all 3 wrappers × all approaches × all features

title: ctrml compact: collapse 3 sparse copies
description:
# tfg.2: ctrml compact branch — collapse 3 sparse copies into one sparseMatrix call
**Status:** `READY_FOR_EXECUTION`

## I. Context & Objective
* **Objective:** Replace ctrml `features="compact"` sel_mat construction (bandSparse → 1*t() → indexed assignment) with a single `Matrix::sparseMatrix(i, j, x, dims=...)` call. Eliminates 2 intermediate sparse copies.
* **Why:** Each R reassignment to a sparse Matrix creates a new dgCMatrix object. For ctrml compact with large hierarchies (nb=100, n*p=2400), each transient copy holds ~p*nb non-zeros + structure overhead.
* **Mechanism:** Derive final (i, j) triples directly from the geometric structure.
* **Forbidden:** Touching csrml/terml/utils/reco_ml; touching ctrml non-compact branches; changing the `block_sampling <- tmp$dim[["m"]]` assignment.
* **Reference Data (verified HEAD adc43a8):**
  - R/ctrml.R:358-369 (ctrml() compact branch):
    ```r
    pos <- seq(
      tmp$dim[["na"]],
      by = tmp$dim[["n"]],
      length.out = tmp$dim[["p"]]
    )
    sel_mat <- Matrix::bandSparse(
      tmp$dim[["nb"]],
      tmp$dim[["n"]] * tmp$dim[["p"]],
      pos
    )
    sel_mat <- 1 * t(sel_mat)
    sel_mat[1:tmp$dim[["n"]], ] <- 1
    block_sampling <- tmp$dim[["m"]]
    ```
  - R/ctrml.R:660-671 (ctrml_fit() compact branch): identical structure, dup site.

## II. Input Specification
R/ctrml.R only.

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Logic** | Final sel_mat byte-identical via `identical(as.matrix(old), as.matrix(new))`. Class `dgCMatrix` preserved (or `dgTMatrix` then `as(. , "CsparseMatrix")` if needed). |
| **Format** | Match style. |
| **Boundary** | R/ctrml.R ONLY. Both compact sites (line 358-369 + 660-671). |
| **API** | None. |
| **block_sampling** | Assignment `block_sampling <- tmp$dim[["m"]]` UNCHANGED, immediately follows new sparseMatrix. |

## IV. Step-by-Step Logic
1. Derive triples analytically:
   - `n   <- tmp$dim[["n"]]`, `nb  <- tmp$dim[["nb"]]`, `na  <- tmp$dim[["na"]]`, `p   <- tmp$dim[["p"]]`.
   - Final dims: `n*p × nb` (after t()).
   - Top n rows × all nb cols: all 1 (from `sel_mat[1:n, ] <- 1`).
     ```r
     i_top <- rep(seq_len(n), times = nb)
     j_top <- rep(seq_len(nb), each = n)
     ```
   - Additional band entries for r in 2..p (the diagonal band rows from bandSparse, after transpose, that land in rows > n):
     For r in 2..p, k in 1..nb: row index = na + (r-1)*n + k, col = k.
     ```r
     if (p > 1) {
       row_offsets <- seq(from = na + n, by = n, length.out = p - 1)   # n+na, 2n+na, ..., (p-1)n+na
       i_band <- as.vector(outer(seq_len(nb), row_offsets, `+`))         # length nb*(p-1)
       j_band <- rep(seq_len(nb), times = p - 1)
     } else {
       i_band <- integer(0); j_band <- integer(0)
     }
     ```
   - Combine + build:
     ```r
     sel_mat <- Matrix::sparseMatrix(
       i = c(i_top, i_band),
       j = c(j_top, j_band),
       x = 1,
       dims = c(n * p, nb)
     )
     ```
2. **MANDATORY** inline byte-identity check during development (NOT committed):
   Build sel_mat both ways for a representative fixture (e.g., nb=8, n=11, na=3, p=4). Compare via `identical(as.matrix(old), as.matrix(new))` and `identical(Matrix::nnzero(old), Matrix::nnzero(new))`. MUST be TRUE before committing.
3. Replace BOTH compact sites in R/ctrml.R with the new construction. Keep `pos <- ...` block removed (no longer needed) but verify nothing else in the file references `pos`.
4. Run `Rscript -e 'devtools::test()'` → 85/85 pass.
5. Numerical equivalence: ctrml(features="compact") × randomForest/xgboost/lightgbm. set.seed(42); max_abs_diff = 0.0 vs baseline HEAD adc43a8.
6. Commit: "tfg.2: ctrml compact sel_mat via single sparseMatrix call (drop 3-copy chain)"

## V. Output Schema
```toon
task_id: tfg.2
success: bool
files_changed: [R/ctrml.R]
sites_migrated: 2
test_result: { passed: int, failed: int }
identity_check: bool       # final sel_mat byte-identical to legacy
numerical_check:
  approaches: [randomForest, xgboost, lightgbm]
  features: [compact]
  max_abs_diff: 0.0
pos_var_removed: bool      # `pos <- seq(...)` no longer needed
```

## VI. Definition of Done
- [ ] Both compact branches (ctrml() + ctrml_fit()) migrated
- [ ] sel_mat byte-identical (verified inline)
- [ ] `Matrix::bandSparse` and `1 * t(...)` and `sel_mat[1:n, ] <- 1` removed from compact branches
- [ ] 85/85 tests pass
- [ ] Numerical reco_mat byte-identical for ctrml compact
close_reason: Landed: 674e07f -> merged 82dadc2. 85/85 tests; 0.0 numerical diff.

title: sel_mat via sparseMatrix() triples (no dense rep)
description:
# tfg.1: sel_mat via sparseMatrix() triples (no dense rep) — 10 sites
**Status:** `READY_FOR_EXECUTION`

## I. Context & Objective
* **Objective:** Replace `Matrix(rep(idx, n_cols), ncol = n_cols, sparse = TRUE)` pattern with `Matrix::sparseMatrix(i, j, x, dims=...)` via a new helper `sparse_col_replicate(idx, n_cols)` in R/utils.R. Total: **10 sites** across csrml/terml/ctrml.
* **Why:** `rep(idx, n_cols)` allocates `length(idx) * n_cols` doubles in one dense vector before R's sparse compression. For ctrml with `length(idx) ~ n*p` and `n_cols = nb*m`, this is the dominant transient.
* **Mechanism:** Single internal helper in R/utils.R (alongside existing cross-file helpers: `sel_mat_keep_cols`, `na_col_mask`, `input2rtw`, `input2rtw_partial`). 10 call sites updated to use the helper.
* **Forbidden:**
  - `Matrix(1, nrow=n, ncol=nb, sparse=TRUE)` "all" sites (csrml.R:214, csrml.R:360) — scalar broadcast, different pattern.
  - `as(rep(...), "sparseVector")` sites in ctrml.R (lines 318, 321, 620, 623) — produces sparseVector, not matrix; different optimization.
  - ctrml compact branch (tfg.2's domain).

## Reference Data (verified HEAD adc43a8)

### 10 target sites:
- **csrml.R** (4 sites, single-line):
  - 203 — `"bts"` feature (csrml)
  - 210 — `"str-bts"` feature (csrml)
  - 349 — `"bts"` (csrml_fit)
  - 356 — `"str-bts"` (csrml_fit)
  All pattern: `Matrix(rep(id_bts, nb), ncol = nb, sparse = TRUE)`.
- **terml.R** (2 sites, single-line, feature `"mfh-str-hfts"` NOT `"mfh-str"`):
  - 232 — terml `"mfh-str-hfts"`
  - 475 — terml_fit `"mfh-str-hfts"`
  Pattern: `Matrix(rep(id_hfts, m), ncol = m, sparse = TRUE)`.
- **ctrml.R** (4 sites, multi-line):
  - 329-333 — `"mfh-str-hfbts"` (ctrml): `Matrix(\n rep(id_hfbts, nb*m), \n ncol=nb*m, sparse=TRUE\n)`
  - 339-346 — `"mfh-str-bts"` (ctrml): `Matrix(\n rep(rep(id_bts, each=kt), nb*m), \n ncol=nb*m, sparse=TRUE\n)` (nested rep)
  - 631-635 — `"mfh-str-hfbts"` (ctrml_fit)
  - 641-648 — `"mfh-str-bts"` (ctrml_fit)

### R/utils.R existing helpers (helper home — verified):
- `sel_mat_keep_cols`, `na_col_mask`, `input2rtw`, `input2rtw_partial`, `new_rml_fit`, `serialize_fit`, `deserialize_fit`, `get_fit_i` — all non-exported, called from csrml/terml/ctrml via namespace visibility. `sparse_col_replicate` joins this family.

## II. Input Specification
R/utils.R (helper definition) + R/csrml.R + R/terml.R + R/ctrml.R (call sites).

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Logic** | New sel_mat byte-identical to legacy at each site via `identical(as.matrix(old), as.matrix(new))`. |
| **Format** | Match snake_case, 2-space indent. |
| **Boundary** | R/utils.R + R/csrml.R + R/terml.R + R/ctrml.R only. NO reco_ml.R, tests, NAMESPACE, DESCRIPTION. |
| **API** | Helper internal (no `@export`). NAMESPACE unchanged. |
| **Strict scope** | Only the 10 listed sites. `Matrix(1, ...)`, `as(..., "sparseVector")`, ctrml compact UNCHANGED. |

## IV. Step-by-Step Logic
1. Add to R/utils.R (next to existing helpers like `sel_mat_keep_cols`):
   ```r
   # Build a column-replicated sparse 0/1 indicator matrix:
   # sparse `length(idx) × n_cols` matrix where each column equals `idx`.
   # Avoids the dense `rep(idx, n_cols)` allocation done by `Matrix(rep(...), sparse=TRUE)`.
   sparse_col_replicate <- function(idx, n_cols) {
     nz <- which(idx != 0)
     Matrix::sparseMatrix(
       i = rep(nz, times = n_cols),
       j = rep(seq_len(n_cols), each = length(nz)),
       x = 1,
       dims = c(length(idx), n_cols)
     )
   }
   ```
2. Replace 10 sites:
   - csrml.R:203 / :210 / :349 / :356 — `sparse_col_replicate(id_bts, nb)` replaces `Matrix(rep(id_bts, nb), ncol=nb, sparse=TRUE)`.
   - terml.R:232 / :475 — `sparse_col_replicate(id_hfts, m)` replaces `Matrix(rep(id_hfts, m), ncol=m, sparse=TRUE)`.
   - ctrml.R:329-333 / :631-635 — `sparse_col_replicate(id_hfbts, tmp$dim[["nb"]] * tmp$dim[["m"]])` replaces the multi-line `Matrix(...)`.
   - ctrml.R:339-346 / :641-648 — precompute `idx_local <- rep(id_bts, each = tmp$dim[["kt"]])` then `sparse_col_replicate(idx_local, tmp$dim[["nb"]] * tmp$dim[["m"]])`.
3. Per-site identity check (inline during development, not committed):
   ```r
   stopifnot(identical(
     as.matrix(Matrix(rep(idx, n_cols), ncol = n_cols, sparse = TRUE)),
     as.matrix(sparse_col_replicate(idx, n_cols))
   ))
   ```
4. Run `Rscript -e 'devtools::test()'` → must show 85/85.
5. Numerical equivalence (set.seed=42, baseline HEAD adc43a8 via git worktree):
   - csrml × {bts, str-bts} × {randomForest, xgboost, lightgbm}: max_abs_diff = 0.0
   - terml × {mfh-str-hfts}: max_abs_diff = 0.0
   - ctrml × {mfh-str-hfbts, mfh-str-bts}: max_abs_diff = 0.0
6. Commit: `git add R/utils.R R/csrml.R R/terml.R R/ctrml.R && git commit -m "tfg.1: sel_mat via sparseMatrix triples helper at 10 sites (drop dense rep)"`

## V. Output Schema
```toon
task_id: tfg.1
success: bool
files_changed: [R/utils.R, R/csrml.R, R/terml.R, R/ctrml.R]
sites_migrated: 10
helper_location: R/utils.R
test_result: { passed: int, failed: int }
identity_check: bool        # all 10 sel_mats byte-identical
numerical_check:
  csrml: { features: [bts, str-bts], approaches: [randomForest, xgboost, lightgbm], max_abs_diff: 0.0 }
  terml: { features: [mfh-str-hfts], approaches: [...], max_abs_diff: 0.0 }
  ctrml: { features: [mfh-str-hfbts, mfh-str-bts], approaches: [...], max_abs_diff: 0.0 }
grep_check:
  matrix_rep_remaining: 0    # `grep -nE "Matrix\\(rep|Matrix\\(\$" R/csrml.R R/terml.R R/ctrml.R` → 0 hits
```

## VI. Definition of Done
- [ ] `sparse_col_replicate()` defined in R/utils.R
- [ ] 10 sites migrated
- [ ] sel_mat byte-identical at each site (verified inline)
- [ ] 85/85 tests pass
- [ ] Numerical reco_mat byte-identical across all 6 wrapper×feature combinations
- [ ] No `Matrix(rep(...)` or multi-line `Matrix(\n rep(...)` pattern remains in csrml/terml/ctrml
close_reason: Landed: b3025ab+338eaa6 -> merged. 85/85 tests; 10 sites; 0.0 diff.

title: Avoid double task build in rml.mlr3 block_sampling
description:
# vu2.1: Build tsk_i once in rml.mlr3 block_sampling branch
**Status:** `READY_FOR_EXECUTION`

## I. Context & Objective
* **Objective:** In `rml.mlr3()`, build `tsk_i` exactly ONCE per call. Currently builds unconditionally first (lines 217-218), then rebuilds inside `block_sampling != NULL` branch (lines 239-240) — discarding the first.
* **Why:** mlr3 `as_task_regr()` deep-copies its data into DataBackendDataTable. Double construction = double training-set transient copy.
* **Mechanism:** Gate the initial build on `is.null(block_sampling)`. When block_sampling set, build the id-augmented form directly (single construction). DROP `rownames(X) <- NULL` (no-op on matrix; redundant per tvy.1b).

## Reference Data (verified HEAD 6c32efe)
R/reco_ml.R lines 215-244 (rml.mlr3 training path), relevant block:
```r
if (is.null(fit)) {
  if (is.null(y) && is.null(X)) cli_abort(...)
  tsk_i <- data.frame(y = y, X, check.names = FALSE)                        # 217 — unconditional first build
  tsk_i <- mlr3::as_task_regr(tsk_i, target = "y")                          # 218
  fit <- do.call(lrn, params)
  if (!is.null(tuning)) {
    ...
    if (!is.null(block_sampling)) {
      rownames(X) <- NULL                                                    # 238 — no-op on matrix
      tsk_i <- data.frame(y = y, X, id = rep(1:NROW(X), each = block_sampling), check.names = FALSE)
      tsk_i <- mlr3::as_task_regr(tsk_i, target = "y")
      tsk_i$col_roles$group <- "id"
      tsk_i$col_roles$feature <- setdiff(tsk_i$col_roles$feature, "id")
      tuning$resampling$instantiate(tsk_i)
    }
    ...
  }
  fit$train(tsk_i)
}
```

## II. Input Specification
R/reco_ml.R ONLY.

## III. Constraints & Guards
| Type | Guard |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Logic** | mlr3 task semantics unchanged: id column has `col_role = "group"`, features exclude id. fit$train(tsk_i) sees identical input. |
| **Boundary** | R/reco_ml.R only. |
| **API** | None. |
| **No double-build** | tsk_i constructed exactly once per training call. |
| **rownames<-NULL drop** | vu2.1 MUST emit a restructured branch WITHOUT `rownames(X) <- NULL`. This is non-negotiable regardless of tvy.1b execution order (see §VII). |

## IV. Step-by-Step Logic
1. Restructure lines 217-240 region so tsk_i is built once:
   ```r
   if (is.null(fit)) {
     if (is.null(y) && is.null(X)) cli_abort(...)

     if (is.null(block_sampling)) {
       tsk_i <- data.frame(y = y, X, check.names = FALSE)
       tsk_i <- mlr3::as_task_regr(tsk_i, target = "y")
     } else {
       tsk_i <- data.frame(
         y = y, X,
         id = rep(seq_len(NROW(X)), each = block_sampling),
         check.names = FALSE
       )
       tsk_i <- mlr3::as_task_regr(tsk_i, target = "y")
       tsk_i$col_roles$group <- "id"
       tsk_i$col_roles$feature <- setdiff(tsk_i$col_roles$feature, "id")
     }

     fit <- do.call(lrn, params)
     if (!is.null(tuning)) {
       # existing tuning setup …
       if (!is.null(block_sampling)) {
         tuning$resampling$instantiate(tsk_i)
       }
       # autotuner …
     }
     fit$train(tsk_i)
   }
   ```
   Note absence of `rownames(X) <- NULL` line.
2. `1:NROW(X)` → `seq_len(NROW(X))` (robustness; safe when NROW=0).
3. col_roles$group + col_roles$feature set INSIDE the new else branch, BEFORE tuning$resampling$instantiate (preserves invariant).
4. Run `Rscript -e 'devtools::test()'` → must show 85/85.
5. Numerical equivalence (mlr3 path, both modes): set.seed(42); max_abs_diff = 0.0 vs baseline HEAD.
6. Commit: "vu2.1: build mlr3 tsk_i once (drop double construct + rownames no-op)"

## V. Output Schema
```toon
task_id: vu2.1
success: bool
files_changed: [R/reco_ml.R]
test_result: { passed: int, failed: int }
tsk_i_builds_per_call: 1
rownames_null_removed: true
numerical_check:
  block_sampling_null: { max_abs_diff: 0.0 }
  block_sampling_set: { max_abs_diff: 0.0 }
```

## VI. Definition of Done
- [ ] tsk_i constructed exactly once per training call
- [ ] block_sampling branch attaches col_roles correctly
- [ ] No `rownames(X) <- NULL` in the restructured branch (`grep -c "rownames(X) <- NULL" R/reco_ml.R` → 0 after this ticket)
- [ ] 85/85 tests pass
- [ ] Numerical mlr3 output unchanged (both block_sampling modes)

## VII. Cross-ticket interaction with tvy.1b
* **vu2.1 unilaterally drops `rownames(X) <- NULL`.** Whether tvy.1b lands before or after vu2.1, the final state has 0 occurrences. Post-vu2.1 grep MUST return 0 hits.
* If tvy.1b lands first: the line is already gone; vu2.1's restructure naturally produces 0 hits.
* If vu2.1 lands first: vu2.1's restructure produces 0 hits; tvy.1b becomes a no-op (its target line is gone) — that ticket may be closed-as-superseded.
close_reason: Landed: 5a7a641+67b6e59 -> merged db589f8. 85/85 tests; legacy semantics preserved (block_sampling no-op without tuning).

title: Tier-B EP: copy elimination
description:
# Epic: Tier-B mw3 copy elimination

## Goal
Drop redundant memory copies on three independent paths: obs/hat unname duplicates (wrapper + rml), fit-reference triple-hold at end of rml() training, deserialized-model accumulation on predict-reuse.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] mw3.1: obs/hat unname copies eliminated or replaced with in-place dimnames<-NULL
- [ ] mw3.2: ml_step intermediate matrix-of-lists removed; bts + fit$fit extracted via direct lapply
- [ ] mw3.3: predict-reuse drops fit_i after bts extraction; p models no longer accumulate on reuse path
- [ ] All 84/84+ tests pass; numerical output byte-identical across all approaches × all features

## Context & Background
Original deferred-findings analysis #11 (obs copies), #14 (fit refs), plus the final-review note on T6 predict-reuse memory accumulation.

Three independent tickets. mw3.3 has highest standalone ROI (finishes T6 story). mw3.1 + mw3.2 are smaller wins, low risk.

## Plan ordering
Sequential, independent: mw3.3 → mw3.2 → mw3.1 (or any order).
Each lands on own branch off main; merge after spec + quality review.

## Sub-Agent Strategy
Subagent-driven development per superpowers/subagent-driven-development.
Each ticket: implementer (sonnet) → spec reviewer → quality reviewer.
close_reason: EP-mw3 copy elimination complete: mw3.1+mw3.2+mw3.3 landed.

title: Tier-B EP: sparse matrix construction
description:
# Epic: Tier-B tfg sparse matrix construction

## Goal
Eliminate dense intermediate allocations in sel_mat construction across csrml/terml/ctrml. Two related fixes: replace `Matrix(rep(...), sparse=TRUE)` pattern with direct sparseMatrix(i,j,x) triples (tfg.1), and collapse ctrml compact's 3-step sparse construction into a single sparseMatrix() call (tfg.2).

## Success Criteria
- [ ] tfg.1: 6 `Matrix(rep(idx, n), ncol=n, sparse=TRUE)` sites in csrml/terml use sparseMatrix(i,j,x,dims) directly; no transient dense rep() allocation.
- [ ] tfg.2: ctrml compact branch builds sel_mat via single sparseMatrix() call (instead of bandSparse → t() → indexed-assignment).
- [ ] sel_mat byte-identical for all touched features × all wrappers.
- [ ] 85/85 tests pass; numerical reco_mat byte-identical.

## Context & Background
Original analysis findings #10 + #12 (B-tier). Both involve sel_mat construction in the wrapper feature switches.

#10 (tfg.1): `Matrix(rep(id_bts, nb), ncol=nb, sparse=TRUE)` allocates a length-(n*nb) dense vector via rep() before sparse coercion. For nb=100, n=200: 20000 doubles allocated transiently. For ctrml-like dims with id_bts of length n*p and ncol=nb*m: much larger.

#12 (tfg.2): ctrml compact branch:
```r
sel_mat <- Matrix::bandSparse(nb, n*p, pos)   # dgCMatrix copy 1
sel_mat <- 1 * t(sel_mat)                      # dgCMatrix copy 2 (transpose + numeric coerce)
sel_mat[1:n, ] <- 1                            # dgCMatrix copy 3 (indexed assignment)
```
Three successive sparse matrix copies via R reassignment semantics. For large hierarchies (nb=100, n*p=2400), each copy holds the sparse structure metadata + final value vector.

## Plan ordering
Independent. tfg.1 + tfg.2 can land in any order. Recommend tfg.2 first (single file, ctrml.R; smaller blast radius) → tfg.1 (3 files).

## Sub-Agent Strategy
Subagent-driven development. Each ticket: implementer (sonnet) → spec reviewer → quality reviewer.
close_reason: EP-tfg sparse construction complete.
